# Authentic hadiths (Sahih al-Bukhari & Sahih Muslim) via fawazahmed0/hadith-api.
# Auto-generated by refresh-hadiths.sh — edits here are overwritten on refresh.
Narrated Anas: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None of you will have faith till he wishes for his (Muslim) brother what he likes for himself  — Bukhari 13
Narrated Anas: The Prophet (ﷺ) said "None of you will have faith till he loves me more than his father, his children and all mankind  — Bukhari 15
Narrated Anas: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Love for the Ansar is a sign of faith and hatred for the Ansar is a sign of hypocrisy  — Bukhari 17
Narrated 'Abdullah: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Abusing a Muslim is Fusuq (an evil doing) and killing him is Kufr (disbelief)  — Bukhari 48
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Once the Prophet (ﷺ) embraced me and said, "O Allah! Bestow on him the knowledge of the Book (Qur'an)  — Bukhari 75
Narrated Abu at-Tufail:The above mentioned Statement of `Ali  — Bukhari 127
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to his wives, "You are allowed to go out to answer the call of nature  — Bukhari 147
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) performed ablution by washing the body parts only once  — Bukhari 157
Narrated `Abdullah bin Zaid:The Prophet (ﷺ) performed ablution by washing the body parts twice  — Bukhari 158
Narrated Anas:When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) got his head shaved, Abu- Talha was the first to take some of his hair  — Bukhari 171
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "If a dog drinks from the utensil of anyone of you it is essential to wash it seven times  — Bukhari 172
Narrated `Abbad bin Tamim:My uncle said: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One should not leave his prayer unless he hears sound or smells something  — Bukhari 177
Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Umar:"During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) men and women used to perform ablution together  — Bukhari 193
Narrated Maimuna:The Prophet (ﷺ) ate (a piece of) mutton from the shoulder region and then prayed without repeating the ablution  — Bukhari 210
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) drank milk, rinsed his mouth and said, "It has fat  — Bukhari 211
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said as above  — Bukhari 221
Narrated `Aishah:I used to wash the semen off the clothes of the Prophet (ﷺ) and even then I used to notice one or more spots on them  — Bukhari 232
Narrated Anas:Prior to the construction of the mosque, the Prophet (ﷺ) offered the prayers at sheep-folds  — Bukhari 234
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) once spat in his clothes  — Bukhari 241
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "All drinks that produce intoxication are Haram (forbidden to drink)  — Bukhari 242
Narrated Hudhaifa:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) got up at night, he used to clean his mouth with Siwak  — Bukhari 245
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) and I used to take a bath from a single pot called 'Faraq  — Bukhari 250
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) and Maimuna used to take a bath from a single pot  — Bukhari 253
Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "As for me, I pour water three times on my head." And he pointed with both his hands  — Bukhari 254
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to pour water three times on his head  — Bukhari 255
Narrated `Aisha:Whenever Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) took a bath of Janaba, he washed his hands first  — Bukhari 262
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) and I used to take a bath from a single pot of water after Janaba  — Bukhari 263
Narrated `Aisha:It is as if I am just looking at the glitter of scent in the parting of the Prophet's head hair while he was a Muhrim  — Bukhari 271
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to visit all his wives in one night and he had nine wives at that time  — Bukhari 284
Narrated `Abdullah:`Umar asked the Prophet (ﷺ) "Can anyone of us sleep while he is Junub?" He replied, "Yes, if he performs ablution  — Bukhari 289
Narrated `Aisha:While in menses, I used to comb the hair of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Bukhari 295
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to lean on my lap and recite Qur'an while I was in menses  — Bukhari 297
Narrated Um `Atiya:We never considered yellowish discharge as a thing of importance (as menses)  — Bukhari 326
Narrated `Umar bin Abi Salama:The Prophet (ﷺ) prayed in one garment and crossed its ends  — Bukhari 354
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None of you should offer prayer in a single garment that does not cover the shoulders  — Bukhari 359
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whoever prays in a single garment must cross its ends (over the shoulders)  — Bukhari 360
Narrated Maimuna:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to pray on Khumra  — Bukhari 381
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Apostle prayed while I was lying like a dead body on his family bed between him and his Qibla  — Bukhari 383
Narrated `Urwa:The Prophet (ﷺ) prayed while `Aisha was lying between him and his Qibla on the bed on which they used to sleep  — Bukhari 384
Narrated Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba: I helped the Prophet (ﷺ) in performing ablution and he passed his wet hands over his Khuffs and prayed  — Bukhari 388
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None of you should spit in front or on his right but he could spit either on his left or under his foot  — Bukhari 412
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Spitting in the mosque is a sin and its expiation is to bury it  — Bukhari 415
Narrated Nafi`:"I saw Ibn `Umar praying while taking his camel as a Sutra in front of him and he said, "I saw the Prophet doing the same  — Bukhari 430
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) had said, "Offer some of your prayers (Nawafil) at home, and do not take your houses as graves  — Bukhari 432
Narrated Nafi`:`Abdullah bin `Umar said: I used to sleep in the mosque of the Prophet (ﷺ) while I was young and unmarried  — Bukhari 440
Narrated Abu Qatada Al-Aslami:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "If anyone of you enters a mosque, he should pray two rak`at before sitting  — Bukhari 444
Narrated Ibn `Umar or Ibn `Amr:The Prophet (ﷺ) clasped his hands, by interlacing his fingers  — Bukhari 478
The narrated Hadith is about the various places on the way from Medina to Mecca where the Prophet (ﷺ) prayed and is not translated  — Bukhari 484
Narrated Salama:The distance between the wall of the mosque and the pulpit was hardly enough for a sheep to pass through  — Bukhari 497
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to get a Harba planted in front of him (as a Sutra) and pray behind it  — Bukhari 498
Narrated Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to offer the `Asr prayer when the sunshine had not disappeared from my chamber  — Bukhari 544
Narrated Salama:We used to pray the Maghrib prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) when the sun disappeared from the horizon  — Bukhari 561
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) prayed seven rak`at together and eight rak`at together  — Bukhari 562
Narrated Abu Barza:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) disliked to sleep before the `Isha' prayer and to talk after it  — Bukhari 568
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whoever could get one rak`a of a prayer, (in its proper time) he has got the prayer  — Bukhari 580
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "None of you should try to pray at sunrise or sunset  — Bukhari 585
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) never missed two rak`at before the Fajr prayer and after the `Asr prayer openly and secretly  — Bukhari 592
Narrated `Aisha:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) come to me after the `Asr prayer, he always prayed two rak`at  — Bukhari 593
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whenever you hear the Adhan, say what the Mu'adh-dhin is saying  — Bukhari 611
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer two light rak`at between the Adhan and the Iqama of the Fajr prayer  — Bukhari 619
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Bilal pronounces the Adhan at night, so eat and drink (Suhur) till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces the Adhan  — Bukhari 622
Narrated `Aun bin Abi Juhaifa:My father said, "I saw Bilal turning his face from side to side while pronouncing the Adhan for the prayer  — Bukhari 634
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Once Iqama was pronounced a man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and detained him (from the prayer)  — Bukhari 643
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If supper is served, and Iqama is pronounced one should start with the supper  — Bukhari 671
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Listen and obey (your chief) even if an Ethiopian whose head is like a raisin were made your chief  — Bukhari 693
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to Abu-Dhar, "Listen and obey (your chief) even if he is an Ethiopian with a head like a raisin  — Bukhari 696
Narrated Mu`adh bin Jabal:I used to pray the `Isha prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and then go to lead my people in the prayer  — Bukhari 700
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to pray a short prayer (in congregation) but used to offer it in a perfect manner  — Bukhari 706
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Mu`adh used to pray with the Prophet (ﷺ) and then go and lead his people (tribe) in the prayer  — Bukhari 711
Narrated An-Nu`man bin 'Bashir:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Straighten your rows or Allah will alter your faces  — Bukhari 717
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Straighten your rows, for I see you from behind my back  — Bukhari 718
Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whoever does not recite Al-Fatiha in his prayer, his prayer is invalid  — Bukhari 756
Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im:My father said, "I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) reciting "at-Tur" (52) in the Maghrib prayer  — Bukhari 765
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) was on a journey and recited in one of the first two rak`at of the `Isha' prayer "Wa t-teeni wa z-zaitun  — Bukhari 767
Narrated Anas:The qunut [supplication before going down for prostration] used to be recited in the Maghrib and the Fajr prayers  — Bukhari 798
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "We have been ordered to prostrates on seven bones and not to tuck up the clothes or hair  — Bukhari 810
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) was ordered to prostrate on seven bony parts and not to tuck up his clothes or hair  — Bukhari 815
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "I have been ordered to prostrate on seven (bones) and not to tuck up the hair or garment  — Bukhari 816
Narrated Malik bin Huwairith Al-Laithi:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) praying and in the odd rak`at, he used to sit for a moment before getting up  — Bukhari 823
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prostrating in mud and water and saw the mark of mud on his forehead  — Bukhari 836
Narrated `Itban bin Malik:We prayed with the Prophet (ﷺ) and used to finish our prayer with the Taslim along with him  — Bukhari 838
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I used to recognize the completion of the prayer of the Prophet (ﷺ) by hearing Takbir  — Bukhari 842
Narrated Samura bin Jundub:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to face us on completion of the prayer  — Bukhari 845
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If your women ask permission to go to the mosque at night, allow them  — Bukhari 865
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Anyone of you attending the Friday (prayers) should take a bath  — Bukhari 877
Narrated Hudhaifa:When the Prophet (ﷺ) got up at night (for the night prayer), he used to clean his mouth  — Bukhari 889
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying, "Anyone of you coming for the Jumua prayer should take a bath  — Bukhari 894
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allow women to go to the Mosques at night  — Bukhari 899
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer the Jumua prayer immediately after midday  — Bukhari 904
Narrated Anas bin Malik:We used to offer the Jumua prayer early and then have an afternoon nap  — Bukhari 905
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to deliver the Khutba while standing and then he would sit, then stand again as you do now-a-days  — Bukhari 920
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:One day the Prophet (ﷺ) sat on the pulpit and we sat around him  — Bukhari 921
Narrated `Abdullah Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to deliver two Khutbas and sit in between them  — Bukhari 928
Narrated Sahl:As above with the addition: We never had an afternoon nap nor meals except after offering the Jumua prayer  — Bukhari 939
Narrated Anas:We used to offer the Jumua prayer early and then have the afternoon nap  — Bukhari 940
Narrated Sahl:We used to offer the Jumua prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and then take the afternoon nap  — Bukhari 941
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), Abu Bakr and `Umar! used to offer the two `Id prayers before delivering the Khutba  — Bukhari 963
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to Nahr or slaughter sacrifices at the Musalla (on `Id-ul-Adha)  — Bukhari 982
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Make witr as your last prayer at night  — Bukhari 998
Narrated Anas:The Qunut used to be recited in the Maghrib and the Fajr prayers  — Bukhari 1004
Narrated `Abbad bin Tamim's uncle:The Prophet (ﷺ) went out to offer the Istisqa' prayer and turned (and put on) his cloak inside out  — Bukhari 1005
Narrated `Abdullah bin Zaid:The Prophet (ﷺ) turned his cloak inside out on Istisqa  — Bukhari 1011
Narrated Aisha:Whenever Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saw the rain, he used to say, "O Allah! Let it be a strong fruitful rain  — Bukhari 1032
Narrated Anas:Whenever a strong wind blew, anxiety appeared on the face of the Prophet (fearing that wind might be a sign of Allah's wrath)  — Bukhari 1034
Narrated Asma:No doubt the Prophet (ﷺ) ordered people to manumit slaves during the solar eclipse  — Bukhari 1054
Narrated Abu Bakra:In the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) the sun eclipsed and then he offered a two rak`at prayer  — Bukhari 1062
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) led us and performed four bowing in two rak`at during the solar eclipse and the first rak`a was longer  — Bukhari 1064
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The prostration of Sa`d is not a compulsory one but I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) prostrating while reciting it  — Bukhari 1069
Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:I recited An-Najm before the Prophet, yet he did not perform a prostration  — Bukhari 1073
Narrated Haritha bin Wahab:The Prophet (ﷺ) led us in the prayer at Mina during the peace period by offering two rak`at  — Bukhari 1083
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A woman should not travel for more than three days except with a Dhi-Mahram  — Bukhari 1087
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer the Nawafil, while riding, facing a direction other than that of the Qibla  — Bukhari 1094
Narrated Salim's father:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer the Maghrib and `Isha' prayers together whenever he was in a hurry on a journey  — Bukhari 1106
Narrated Anas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to offer these two prayers together on journeys i.e. the Maghrib and the `Isha  — Bukhari 1108
Narrated Jundab:The Prophet (ﷺ) became sick and did not get up (for Tahajjud prayer) for a night or two  — Bukhari 1124
Narrated Al-Ashath:He (the Prophet (ﷺ) ) used to get up for the prayer on hearing the crowing of a cock  — Bukhari 1132.2
Narrated `Aisha:In my house he (Prophet (ﷺ) ) never passed the last hours of the night but sleeping  — Bukhari 1133
Narrated Hudhaifa:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) got up for Tahajjud prayer he used to clean his mouth (and teeth) with Siwak  — Bukhari 1136
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The prayer of the Prophet (ﷺ) used to be of thirteen rak`at, i.e. of the night prayer  — Bukhari 1138
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to lie down on his right side, after offering two rak`at (Sunna) of the Fajr prayer  — Bukhari 1160
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) was never more regular and particular in offering any Nawafil than the two rak`at (Sunna) of the Fajr prayer  — Bukhari 1163
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) led us and offered a two rak`at prayer and then went away  — Bukhari 1168
Narrated `Aisha:I never saw the Prophet (ﷺ) offering the Duha prayer but I always offer it  — Bukhari 1177
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) never missed four rak`at before the Zuhr prayer and two rak`at before the Fajr prayer  — Bukhari 1182
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Offer some of your prayers in your houses and do not make them graves  — Bukhari 1187
Narrated `Abdullah:The same as No. 290. from the Prophet  — Bukhari 1199.2
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The saying 'Subhan Allah' is for men and clapping is for women  — Bukhari 1204
Narrated Mu'aiqib:The Prophet (ﷺ) talked about a man leveling the earth on prostrating, and said, "If you have to do so, then do it once  — Bukhari 1207
Narrated Abu Huraira:It was forbidden to keep the hands on the hips during the prayer. (This is narrated by Abu Huraira from the Prophet)  — Bukhari 1219
Narrated Abu Huraira:It was forbidden to pray with the hands over one's hips  — Bukhari 1220
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was shrouded in three pieces of cloth and neither a shirt nor a turban were used  — Bukhari 1272
Narrated Um 'Atiyya:We were forbidden to accompany funeral processions but not strictly  — Bukhari 1278
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The real patience is at the first stroke of a calamity  — Bukhari 1302
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) offered the funeral prayer for An-Najashi and I was in the second or third row  — Bukhari 1317
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) offered the funeral prayer of As-Hama An-Najash and said four Takbir  — Bukhari 1334
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) buried every two martyrs in of Uhud in one grave  — Bukhari 1345
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:My mother and I were among the weak and oppressed. I from among the children, and my mother from among the women  — Bukhari 1357
Narrated Al-Bara':When Ibrahim (the son of Prophet) expired, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There is a wet-nurse for him in Paradise  — Bukhari 1382
Narrated Abu Bakr bin `Aiyash:Sufyan at-Tammar told me that he had seen the grave of the Prophet (ﷺ) elevated and convex  — Bukhari 1390.2
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Don't abuse the dead, because they have reached the result of what they forwarded  — Bukhari 1393
Narrated `Adi bin Hatim heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying:"Save yourself from Hell-fire even by giving half a date-fruit in charity  — Bukhari 1417
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There is no Zakat either on a horse or a slave belonging to a Muslim  — Bukhari 1463
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There is no Zakat either on a slave or on a horse belonging to a Muslim  — Bukhari 1464
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) had forbidden the sale of fruits till they were ripe (free from blight)  — Bukhari 1487
Narrated Abu Sa`id:We used to give one Sa' of barley as Sadaqat-ul-Fitr (per head)  — Bukhari 1505
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) ordered the people to pay Zakat-ul-Fitr before going to the `Id prayer  — Bukhari 1509
Narrated Salim from his father who said:"The Prophet (ﷺ) had fixed the Mawaqit as follows: (No)  — Bukhari 1527
Narrated Salim from his father:I heard that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) assumed Ihram with his hair matted together  — Bukhari 1540
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) recited Talbiya when he had mounted his Mount and was ready to set out  — Bukhari 1552
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to enter Mecca from the high Thaniya and used to leave Mecca from the low Thaniya  — Bukhari 1575
Narrated `Aisha:When the Prophet (ﷺ) came to Mecca he entered from its higher side and left from its lower side  — Bukhari 1577
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet;; said, "Dhus-Suwaiqa-tain (literally: One with two lean legs) from Ethiopia will demolish the Ka`ba  — Bukhari 1591
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Dhus-Suwaiqatain (the thin legged man) from Ethiopia will demolish the Ka`ba  — Bukhari 1596
Narrated `Abdullah:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) forbidding the offering of prayers at the time of sunrise and sunset  — Bukhari 1629
Narrated Abu Aiyub Al-Ansari:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) offered the Maghrib and `Isha' prayers together at Al-Muzdalifa  — Bukhari 1674
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had sent me from Jam' (i.e. Al-Muzdalifa) at night  — Bukhari 1677
Narrated `Aisha:Once the Prophet (ﷺ) sent sheep as Hadi  — Bukhari 1701
Narrated Aisha:I used to twist the garlands for the sheep of the Prophet (ﷺ) and he would send them (to the Ka`ba), and stay as a non-Muhrim  — Bukhari 1703
Narrated `Aisha:I twisted (the garlands) for the Hadis of the Prophet (ﷺ) before he assumed Ihram  — Bukhari 1704
Narrated `Aisha:I twisted the garlands of the Hadis from the wool which was with me  — Bukhari 1705
Narrated `Ali:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) ordered me to give in charity the skin and the coverings of the Budn which I had slaughtered  — Bukhari 1707
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) (got) his head shaved after performing his Hajj  — Bukhari 1726
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) and some of his companions got their heads shaved and some others got their hair cut short  — Bukhari 1729
Narrated Muawiya:I cut short the hair of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) with a long blade  — Bukhari 1730
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) delivering a sermon at `Arafat  — Bukhari 1740
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) permitted the people who provided the pilgrims with water to stay at Mecca during the nights of Mina  — Bukhari 1743
Narrated Ibn `Umar:That the Prophet (ﷺ) allowed people who provided the pilgrims with water to stay at Mecca during the nights of Mina  — Bukhari 1744
Narrated `Aisha:It (i.e. Al-Abtah) was a place where the Prophet (ﷺ) used to camp so that it might be easier for him to depart  — Bukhari 1765
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade going to one's family at night (on arrival from a journey)  — Bukhari 1801
Narrated Nafi`:Some of the sons of `Abdullah told him (i.e. `Abdullah) if he had stayed (and not performed Hajj that year)  — Bukhari 1808
Narrated Ka'b bin 'Ujra (ra):Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saw him (i.e. Ka'b) while the lice were falling on his face  — Bukhari 1818
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "It is not sinful of a Muhrim to kill five kinds of animals  — Bukhari 1826
One of the wives of the Prophet (ﷺ) narrated:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A Muhrim can kill (five kinds of animals)  — Bukhari 1827
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was cupped while he was in a state of Ihram  — Bukhari 1835
Narrated Ibn Buhaina:The Prophet, while in the state of Ihram, was cupped at the middle of his head at Liha-Jamal  — Bukhari 1836
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) married Maimuna while he was in the state of Ihram, (only the ceremonies of marriage were held)  — Bukhari 1837
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) sent me (to Mina) with the luggage from Jam' (i.e. Al-Muzdalifa) at night  — Bukhari 1856
Narrated Abu Humaid:We came with the Prophet (ﷺ) from Tabuk, and when we reached near Medina, the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "This is Tabah  — Bukhari 1872
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Bestow on Medina twice the blessings You bestowed on Mecca  — Bukhari 1885
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "When Ramadan begins, the gates of Paradise are opened  — Bukhari 1898
Narrated Abu Bakra:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The two months of `Id i.e. Ramadan and Dhul-Hijja, do not decrease (in superiority)  — Bukhari 1912
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Take Suhur as there is a blessing in it  — Bukhari 1923
Narrated Hisham's father:Aisha said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to kiss some of his wives while he was fasting," and then she smiled  — Bukhari 1928
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) was cupped while he was in the state of Ihram, and also while he was observing a fast  — Bukhari 1938
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) was cupped while he was fasting  — Bukhari 1939
Narrated `Aisha:Hamza bin `Amr Al-Aslami said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I fast continuously  — Bukhari 1942
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The people will remain on the right path as long as they hasten the breaking of the fast  — Bukhari 1957
Narrated Abu Huraira:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "None of you should fast on Friday unless he fasts a day before or after it  — Bukhari 1985
Narrated `Aisha and Ibn `Umar:Nobody was allowed to fast on the days of Tashriq except those who could not afford the Hadi (Sacrifice)  — Bukhari 1997
Narrated Salim's father:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever wishes may fast on the day of 'Ashura  — Bukhari 2000
Narrated `Aisha:(the wife of the Prophet) Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to pray (at night) in Ramadan  — Bukhari 2011
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Search for the Night of Qadr in the odd nights of the last ten days of Ramadan  — Bukhari 2017
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Look for (the Night of Qadr)  — Bukhari 2019
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to practice I`tikaf in the last ten days of the month of Ramadan  — Bukhari 2025
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A time will come when one will not care how one gains one's money, legally or illegally  — Bukhari 2059
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) purchased food grains from a Jew on credit and mortgaged his iron armor to him  — Bukhari 2068
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The Prophet David (ﷺ) used not to eat except from the earnings of his manual labor  — Bukhari 2073
Narrated Az-Zubair bin Al-Awwam:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One would rather take a rope and cut wood and carry it than ask others  — Bukhari 2075
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) bought food grains from a Jew on credit and mortgaged his armor to him  — Bukhari 2096
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Bani Najjar! Suggest a price for your garden." Part of it was a ruin and it contained some date palms  — Bukhari 2106
Narrated Al-Miqdam bin Ma'diyakrib:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Measure your foodstuff and you will be blessed  — Bukhari 2128
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who buys foodstuff should not sell it till he has received it  — Bukhari 2133
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade Najsh  — Bukhari 2142
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade selling by Mulamasa and Munabadha  — Bukhari 2146
Narrated Abu Sa`id:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade two kinds of dresses and two kinds of sale, i.e., Mulamasa and Munabadha  — Bukhari 2147
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the selling of the goods of a desert dweller by a town person  — Bukhari 2159
Narrated Anas bin Malik:We were forbidden that a town dweller should sell goods of a desert dweller  — Bukhari 2161
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade Muzabana and Muhaqala  — Bukhari 2187
Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) allowed the owner of 'Araya to sell the fruits on the trees by means of estimation  — Bukhari 2188
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the sale of (date) fruits till they were red or yellow and fit for eating  — Bukhari 2196
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) bought some foodstuff from a Jew on credit and mortgaged his armor to him  — Bukhari 2200
Narrated Anas:Amongst the captives was Safiya. First she was given to Dihya Al-Kalbi and then to the Prophet  — Bukhari 2228
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) sold a Mudabbar (on behalf of his master who was still living and in need of money)  — Bukhari 2230
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) sold a Mudabbar  — Bukhari 2231
Narrated Ibn Abi Najih:as above, mentioning only specific measure  — Bukhari 2239.2
Narrated Ibn Abi Najih:as above, saying, "He should pay the price in advance for a specified measure and for a specified period  — Bukhari 2240.2
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:When the Prophet (ﷺ) was cupped, he paid the man who cupped him his wages  — Bukhari 2278
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to get cupped and would never withhold the wages of any person  — Bukhari 2280
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) prohibited the earnings of slave girls (through prostitution)  — Bukhari 2283
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade taking a price for animal copulation  — Bukhari 2284
Narrated Anas:`Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf came to us and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) established a bond of brotherhood between him and Sa`d bin Rabi`a  — Bukhari 2293
Narrated `Ali:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) ordered me to distribute the saddles and skins of the Budn which I had slaughtered  — Bukhari 2299
Narrated Abu Huraira:that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do not withhold the superfluous water in order to withhold the superfluous grass  — Bukhari 2354
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One of the rights of a she camel is that it should be milked at a place of water  — Bukhari 2378
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Procrastination (delay) in repaying debts by a wealthy person is injustice  — Bukhari 2400
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Help your brother, whether he is an oppressor or he is an oppressed one  — Bukhari 2443
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Oppression will be a darkness on the Day of Resurrection  — Bukhari 2447
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The most hated person in the sight of Allah is the most quarrelsome person  — Bukhari 2457
Narrated Hudhaifa:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) coming (or the Prophet (ﷺ) came) to the dumps of some people and urinated there while standing  — Bukhari 2471
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) judged that seven cubits should be left as a public way when there was a dispute about the land  — Bukhari 2473
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr bin Al-`As:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "Whoever is killed while protecting his property then he is a martyr  — Bukhari 2480
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) bought some foodstuff on credit for a limited period and mortgaged his armor for it  — Bukhari 2509
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) bought some foodstuff from a Jew and mortgaged his armor to him  — Bukhari 2513
Narrated Asma' bint Abu Bakr:The Prophet (ﷺ) ordered us to free slaves at the time of solar eclipses  — Bukhari 2519
Narrated Asma' bint Abu Bakr:We were ordered to free slaves at the time of lunar eclipses  — Bukhari 2520
Narrated Abu Huraira:That the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever frees his portion of a (common) slave  — Bukhari 2526
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the selling or donating the Wala' of a freed slave  — Bukhari 2535
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If somebody fights (or beats somebody) then he should avoid the face  — Bukhari 2559
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to accept gifts and used to give something in return  — Bukhari 2585
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One who takes back his gift (which he has already given) is like a dog that swallows its vomit  — Bukhari 2589
Jabir (ra) said, "I went to the Prophet (ﷺ) in the mosque and he paid me my right and gave me more than he owed me  — Bukhari 2603
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who takes back his present is like him who swallows his vomit  — Bukhari 2621
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) gave the verdict that `Umra is for the one to whom it is presented  — Bukhari 2625
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Umra is permissible." Ata said, "Jabir narrated the same to me from the Prophet  — Bukhari 2626
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Bath on Friday is compulsory for those who have attained the age of puberty  — Bukhari 2665
Narrated Ibn Abu Mulaika:Ibn `Abbas wrote that the Prophet (ﷺ) gave his verdict on the basis of the defendant's oath  — Bukhari 2668
Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi `Aufa:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Know that Paradise is under the shades of swords  — Bukhari 2818
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Plague is the cause of martyrdom of every Muslim (who dies because of it)  — Bukhari 2830
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) went on carrying (i.e. the earth) and saying, "Without You (O Allah!) we would have got no guidance  — Bukhari 2836
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There is a blessing in the fore-heads of horses  — Bukhari 2851
Narrated Sahl:In our garden there was a horse belonging to the Prophet (ﷺ) called Al-Luhaif or Al-Lukhaif  — Bukhari 2855
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying. "Evil omen is in three things: The horse, the woman and the house  — Bukhari 2858
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) fixed two shares for the horse and one share for its rider (from the war booty)  — Bukhari 2863
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) met them (i.e. the people) while he was riding an unsaddled horse with his sword slung over his shoulder  — Bukhari 2866
Narrated Anas:The she camel of the Prophet (ﷺ) was called Al-Adba  — Bukhari 2871
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) died while his (iron) armor was mortgaged to a Jew for thirty Sas of barley  — Bukhari 2916
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf and Az-Zubair bin Al-`Awwam to wear silk  — Bukhari 2921
Narrated Anas:(Wearing of silk) was allowed to them (i.e. `AbdurRahman and Az-Zubair) because of the itching they suffered from  — Bukhari 2922
Ka`b bin Malik used to say:"Scarcely did Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) set out for a journey on a day other than Thursday  — Bukhari 2949
Narrated Nafi bin Jubair: I heard Al Abbas telling Az-Zubair, "The Prophet (ﷺ) ordered you to fix the flag here  — Bukhari 2976
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the people to travel to a hostile country carrying (copies of) the Qur'an  — Bukhari 2990
Narrated Jabir:Whenever we went up a place we would say Takbir, and whenever we went down we would say, "Subhan Allah  — Bukhari 2994
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah wonders at those people who will enter Paradise in chains  — Bukhari 3010
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) burnt the date-palms of Bani An-Nadir  — Bukhari 3021
Narrated Abu Huraira: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Do not wish to meet the enemy, but when you meet face) the enemy, be patient  — Bukhari 3026
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) called,: "War is deceit  — Bukhari 3029
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "War is deceit  — Bukhari 3030
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Free the captives, feed the hungry and pay a visit to the sick  — Bukhari 3046
Narrated Jubair:(who was among the captives of the Battle of Badr) I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) reciting 'Surat-at-Tur' in the Maghrib prayer  — Bukhari 3050
Narrated Abu Talha:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) conquered some people, he would stay in their town for three days  — Bukhari 3065
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) performed `Umra, setting out from Al-Ji'rana where he distributed the war booty of Hunain  — Bukhari 3066
Narrated As-Sa'ib bin Yazid:I along with some boys went out to receive Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) at Thaniyatal-Wada  — Bukhari 3083
Narrated Jabir:Once I returned from a journey and the Prophet (ﷺ) said (to me) "Offer two rak`at." (Sirar is a place near Medina)  — Bukhari 3090
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Booty has been made legal for me  — Bukhari 3122
Narrated Ibn `Umar:In our holy battles, we used to get honey and grapes, as war booty which we would eat and would not store  — Bukhari 3154
Narrated Aisha:Once the Prophet (ﷺ) was bewitched so that he began to imagine that he had done a thing which in fact he had not done  — Bukhari 3175
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The sun and the moon will be folded up (deprived of their light) on the Day of Resurrection  — Bukhari 3200
Narrated Al Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to Hassan, "Lampoon them (i.e. the pagans) and Gabriel is with you  — Bukhari 3213
Narrated Al-Bara (bin Azib):The Prophet, after the death of his son Ibrahim, said, "There is a wet-nurse for him (i.e. Ibrahim) in Paradise  — Bukhari 3255
Narrated Rafi` bin Khadij:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "Fever is from the heat of the (Hell) Fire; so cool it with water  — Bukhari 3262
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Fever is from the heat of the (Hell) Fire, so cool it with water  — Bukhari 3263
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Fever is from the heat of the (Hell) Fire; so abate fever with water  — Bukhari 3264
Narrated Ibn 'Umar (ra):Abu Lubaba and Zaid bin Khattab saw me  — Bukhari 3299
Narrated Um Sharik:That the Prophet (ﷺ) ordered her to kill Salamanders  — Bukhari 3307
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Kill the snake with two white lines on its back, for it blinds the on-looker and causes abortion  — Bukhari 3308
Narrated Abu Talha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Angels do not enter a house that has either a dog or a picture in it  — Bukhari 3322
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) ordered that the dogs should be killed  — Bukhari 3323
Narrated `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) recited the following Verse) in the usual tone: 'Fahal-Min-Muddalkir  — Bukhari 3341
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Abraham did not tell a lie except on three occasions  — Bukhari 3357
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "May Allah forgive Lot: He wanted to have a powerful support  — Bukhari 3375
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) recited:-- 'Hal-min-Muddakir' (54.15) (Is there any that will remember) (and avoid evil)  — Bukhari 3376
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None of you should say that I am better than Yunus (i.e. Jonah)." Musadded added, "Jonah bin Matta  — Bukhari 3412
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None should say that I am better than Yunus bin Matta  — Bukhari 3416
Narrated `Aisha:That she used to hate that one should keep his hands on his flanks while praying. She said that the Jews used to do so  — Bukhari 3458
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Authority of ruling will remain with Quraish, even if only two of them remained  — Bukhari 3501
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "May Allah save the tribe of Aslam, and may Allah forgive the tribe of Ghifar  — Bukhari 3514
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "`Amr bin Luhai bin Qam'a bin Khindif was the father of Khuza`a  — Bukhari 3520
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) died when he was sixty three years old  — Bukhari 3536
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Name yourselves after me, but do not call yourselves by my Kuniya  — Bukhari 3538
Narrated Abu Huraira:Abu-l-Qasim said, "Name yourselves after me, but do not call yourselves by my Kuniya  — Bukhari 3539
Narrated Abu Juhaifa:I saw the Prophet, and Al-Hasan resembled him  — Bukhari 3543
Narrated Wahb Abu Juhaifa As-Suwai:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) and saw some white hair below his lower lip above the chin  — Bukhari 3545
Narrated Al-Bara:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was the handsomest of all the people, and had the best appearance. He was neither very tall nor short  — Bukhari 3549
Narrated Abu 'Is-haq:Al-Bara' was asked, "Was the face of the Prophet (as bright) as a sword?" He said, "No, but (as bright) as a moon  — Bukhari 3552
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:The Prophet (ﷺ) was shier than a veiled virgin girl  — Bukhari 3562
Narrated Hudhaifa:My companions learned (something about) good (through asking the Prophet) while I learned (something about) evil  — Bukhari 3607
Narrated Anas:That the Meccan people requested Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) to show them a miracle, and so he showed them the splitting of the moon  — Bukhari 3637
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The moon was split into two parts during the lifetime of the Prophet  — Bukhari 3638
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There is always goodness in horses till the Day of Resurrection  — Bukhari 3644
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There is always goodness in horses  — Bukhari 3645
Narrated `Abdullah:We have been powerful since `Umar embraced Islam  — Bukhari 3684
Narrated `Abdullah bin Hisham:We were with the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was holding `Umar bin Al-Khattab by the hand  — Bukhari 3694
And narrated Sad that the Prophet (ﷺ) said to 'Ali, "Will you not be pleased from this that you are to me like Aaron was to Moses?  — Bukhari 3706
Abu Bakr said: Look at Muhammad through his family (i.e. if you are no good to his family you are not good to him)  — Bukhari 3713
Narrated Al-Miswar bin Makhrama:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Fatima is a part of me, and he who makes her angry, makes me angry  — Bukhari 3714
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Every prophet used to have a Hawari (i.e. disciple), and my Hawari is Az-Zubair bin Al-`Awwam  — Bukhari 3719
Narrated Qais bin Abi Hazim:I saw Talha's paralyzed hand with which he had protected the Prophet (from an arrow)  — Bukhari 3724
Narrated Sa`d:No doubt, (for some time) I stood for one-third of the Muslims  — Bukhari 3726
Narrated Ibn `Umar from Hafsa his sister:That the Prophet (ﷺ) had said to her, "`Abdullah is a pious man  — Bukhari 3740
Narrated Al-Bara:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) carrying Al-Hasan on his shoulder an saying, "O Allah! I love him, so please love him  — Bukhari 3749
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Abu Bakr used to say, "Look after Muhammad (ﷺ) in (looking after) his family  — Bukhari 3751
Narrated Anas:None resembled the Prophet (ﷺ) more than Al-Hasan bin `Ali did  — Bukhari 3752
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:`Umar used to say, "Abu Bakr is our chief, and he manumitted our chief," meaning Bilal  — Bukhari 3754
Narrated Al-Miswar bin Makhrama:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Fatima is a part of me, and whoever makes her angry, makes me angry  — Bukhari 3767
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The sign of Belief is to love the Ansar, and the sign of hypocrisy is to hate the Ansar  — Bukhari 3784
Narrated Husain:That `Ikrima said, "Kasan Dihaqa means glass full (of something) followed successively with other full glasses  — Bukhari 3839
Ibn `Abbas said:"In the pre-Islamic period of ignorance I heard my father saying, "Provide us with Kasan Dihaqa  — Bukhari 3840
Narrated `Ammar bin Yasir:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) , and the only converts (to Islam) with him, were five slaves, two women and Abu Bakr  — Bukhari 3857
Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas'ud:We have been powerful since `Umar embraced Islam  — Bukhari 3863
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas:During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) the moon was split (into two pieces)  — Bukhari 3870
Narrated `Abdullah:The moon was split (into two pieces)  — Bukhari 3871
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) offered the funeral prayer for Ashama, the Negus, with four Takbir  — Bukhari 3879
Narrated Jabir:My father, my two maternal uncles and I were among those who took part in the 'Aqaba Pledge  — Bukhari 3891
Narrated Mujahid bin Jabir Al-Makki:`Abdullah bin `Umar used to say, "There is no more Hijrah (i.e. migration) after the Conquest of Mecca  — Bukhari 3899
Narrated Salman Al-Farisi:That he was sold (as a slave) by one master to another for more than ten times (i.e. between 13 and)  — Bukhari 3946
Narrated Salman:I am from Ram-Hurmuz (i.e. a Persian town)  — Bukhari 3947
Narrated Salman:The interval between Jesus and Muhammad was six hundred years  — Bukhari 3948
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The believers who failed to join the Ghazwa of Badr and those who took part in it are not equal (in reward)  — Bukhari 3954
Narrated Al-Bara:I and Ibn `Umar were considered too young to take part in the battle of Badr  — Bukhari 3955
Narrated Anas bin Malik:(as above Hadith)  — Bukhari 3963.2
Narrated `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf:(the grandfather of Salih bin Ibrahim) the story of Badr, namely, the narration regarding the sons of 'Afra  — Bukhari 3964
Narrated Mu`adh:The one who asked (the Prophet) was Gabriel (refer to Hadith)  — Bukhari 3994
Narrated Anas:Abu Zaid died and did not leave any offspring, and he was one of the Badr warriors  — Bukhari 3996
Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit:(who was one of the Badr warriors) Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Give me the pledge of allegiance  — Bukhari 3999
Narrated Ibn Ma`qil:`Ali led the funeral prayer of Sahl bin Hunaif and said, "He was one of the warriors of Badr  — Bukhari 4004
Narrated Abu Masud Al-Badri:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A man's spending on his family is a deed of charity  — Bukhari 4006
Narrated Abu Masud Al-Badri:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "It is sufficient for one to recite the last two Verses of Surat-al-Baqara at night  — Bukhari 4008
Narrated `Abdullah bin Shaddad bin Al-Had Al-Laithi:I saw Rifa`a bin Rafi` Al-Ansari who was a Badr warrior  — Bukhari 4014
Narrated Az-Zubair:On the day of Badr, (Quraishi) Emigrants received 100 shares of the war booty  — Bukhari 4027
Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:I mentioned to Ibn `Abbas Surat-Hashr. He said, "Call it Surat-an-Nadir  — Bukhari 4029
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:On the day of Uhud. the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "This is Gabriel holding the head of his horse and equipped with war material  — Bukhari 4041
Narrated Sa`d:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) mentioned both his father and mother for me on the day of the battle of Uhud  — Bukhari 4056
Narrated `Ali:I have never heard the Prophet (ﷺ) mentioning both his father and mother for anybody other than Sa`d  — Bukhari 4058
Narrated Mu'tamir's father:`Uthman said that on the day of the battle of Uhud, none remained with the Prophet (ﷺ) but Talha and Sa`d  — Bukhari 4060
Narrated Qais:I saw Talha's paralyzed hand with which he had protected the Prophet (ﷺ) on the day of Uhud  — Bukhari 4063
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "This is a mountain that loves us and is loved by us  — Bukhari 4083
Narrated Jabir:The person who killed Khubaib was Abu Sirwa' (i.e. `Uqba bin Al-Harith)  — Bukhari 4087
Narrated Anas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said Al-Qunut for one month after the posture of Bowing, invoking evil upon some 'Arab tribes  — Bukhari 4089
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The first day (i.e. Ghazwa) I participated in, was the day of Al-Khandaq (i.e. Trench)  — Bukhari 4107
Narrated Sinan and Abu Salama:Jabir mentioned that he had participated in a Ghazwa towards Najd in the company of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Bukhari 4134
Narrated Ibn `Umar:On the day of Khaiber, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the eating of garlic and the meat of donkeys  — Bukhari 4215
Narrated Ibn `Umar:On the day of Khaibar, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the eating of donkey meat  — Bukhari 4217
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the eating of donkey-meat  — Bukhari 4218
Narrated Al-Bara:We took part in a Ghazwa with the Prophet (same as Hadith No)  — Bukhari 4225
Narrated `Aisha:When Khaibar was conquered, we said, "Now we will eat our fill of dates  — Bukhari 4242
Narrated Ibn `Umar:We did not eat our fill except after we had conquered Khaibar  — Bukhari 4243
Abu Sa`id and Abu Huraira said:"The Prophet (ﷺ) made the brother of Bani Adi from the Ansar as the ruler of Khaibar  — Bukhari 4246
Narrated Abu Huraira:When Khaibar was conquered, a (cooked) sheep containing poison, was given as a present to Allah's Apostle  — Bukhari 4249
Narrated Khalid bin Al-Walid:On the day of Mu'tah, nine swords were broken in my hand and only a Yemenite sword of mine remained in my hand  — Bukhari 4266
Ibn `Abbas added:"The Prophet (ﷺ) went (to Hunain) in the year of the Conquest (of Mecca)  — Bukhari 4278
Narrated `Aisha:During the year of the Conquest (of Mecca), the Prophet (ﷺ) entered Mecca through Kada which was at the upper part of Mecca  — Bukhari 4290
Narrated Hisham's father:During the year of the Conquest (of Mecca), the Prophet (ﷺ) entered Mecca through its upper part through Kada  — Bukhari 4291
Narrated Anas:We stayed (in Mecca) for ten days along with the Prophet (ﷺ) and used to offer shortened prayers (i.e. journey prayers)  — Bukhari 4297
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) stayed in Mecca for 19 days during which he prayed 2 rak`at in each prayer  — Bukhari 4298
Narrated `Abdullah bin Tha`laba bin Su`air whose face was rubbed by the Prophet during the year of the Conquest (of Makkah)  — Bukhari 4300
(In another narration) Ibn `Umar said:"There is no migration today or after Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)." (and completed his statement as above)  — Bukhari 4310
Narrated Mujahid bin Jabr:`Abdullah bin `Umar used to say, "There is no migration after the Conquest (of Mecca)  — Bukhari 4311
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) got his head shaved during Hajjat-ul-Wada  — Bukhari 4410
Narrated As-Sa'ib bin Yazid:I remember that I went out with the boys to (the place called) Thaniyat-ul-Wada` to receive Allah's Apostle  — Bukhari 4426
Narrated `Aisha:When the Prophet (ﷺ) fell ill in his fatal illness, he started saying, "With the highest companion  — Bukhari 4436
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) died while he was between my chest and chin, so I never dislike the death agony for anyone after the Prophet  — Bukhari 4446
Narrated Aisha and Ibn `Abbas:Abu Bakr kissed the Prophet (ﷺ) after his death  — Bukhari 4455
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Apostle died when he was sixty-three years of age  — Bukhari 4466
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) died while his armor was mortgaged to a Jew for thirty Sa's of barley  — Bukhari 4467
Narrated Al-Bara:I fought fifteen Ghazawat in the company of the Prophet  — Bukhari 4472
Narrated Buraida:That he fought sixteen Ghazawat with Allah's Apostle  — Bukhari 4473
Narrated Anas:None remains of those who prayed facing both Qiblas (that is, Jerusalem and Mecca) except myself  — Bukhari 4489
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The prescribed Law of Allah is the equality in punishment (i.e. Al-Qisas)." (In cases of murders, etc)  — Bukhari 4499
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The most hated man in the Sight of Allah is the one who is the most quarrelsome  — Bukhari 4523
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The last Verse (in the Qur'an) revealed to the Prophet (ﷺ) was the Verse dealing with usury (i.e. Riba)  — Bukhari 4544
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:My mother and I were among the weak and oppressed (Muslims at Mecca)  — Bukhari 4587
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Not equal are those believers who sat (at home) and did not join the Badr battle and those who joined the Badr battle  — Bukhari 4595
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:'"Except the weak ones" (4.98) and added: My mother was one of those whom Allah excused  — Bukhari 4597
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None has the right to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta  — Bukhari 4603
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever says that I am better than Jonah bin Matta, is a liar  — Bukhari 4604
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Nobody has the rights to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta  — Bukhari 4630
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Nobody has the right to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta  — Bukhari 4631
Abdullah bin Az-Zubair said:Allah ordered His Prophet to forgive the people their misbehavior (towards him)  — Bukhari 4644
Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:I asked Ibn `Abbas regarding Surat-al-Anfal. He said, "It was revealed in connection with the Battle of Badr  — Bukhari 4645
Narrated Abu Wail:`Abdullah bin Mas`ud recited "Haita laka (Come you)," and added, "We recite it as we were taught it  — Bukhari 4692
Narrated Ibn Mas`ud:Surat Bani Israel and Al-Kahf and Mary are among my first old property  — Bukhari 4708
Narrated Um Ruman:Aisha's mother, When `Aisha was accused, she fell down Unconscious  — Bukhari 4751
Narrated Ibn Abi Mulaika:I heard `Aisha reciting: "When you invented a lie (and carry it) on your tongues  — Bukhari 4752
Narrated `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Nobody has the right to be better than (Jonah) bin Matta  — Bukhari 4804
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who says that I am better than Jonah bin Matta, tells a lie  — Bukhari 4805
Narrated `Abdullah:Five things have passed: Al-Lizam, the defeat of the Romans, the mighty grasp, the splitting of the moon, and the smoke  — Bukhari 4825
Narrated Abu Huraira:(As above, No. 354, but added) Then Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Recite if you wish: "Would you then  — Bukhari 4831
Narrated Muawiya bin Abi Al-Muzarrad:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), said, "Recite if you wish: Would you then if you were given the authority  — Bukhari 4832
Narrated Anas:"Verily, We have given you (O Muhammad) a manifest victory.' refers to Al-Hudaibiya Peace treaty  — Bukhari 4834
Narrated Jabir:We were one thousand and four hundred on the Day of Al-Hudaibiya  — Bukhari 4840
Abdullah bin Al-Mughaffal Al-Muzani also said:"The Prophet (ﷺ) also forbade urinating at the place where one takes a bath  — Bukhari 4842
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:(regarding His Statement about the Lat and the `Uzza: Lat was originally a man who used to mix Sawiq for the pilgrim)  — Bukhari 4859
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The moon was cleft asunder during the lifetime of the Prophet  — Bukhari 4866
Narrated Anas:The moon was cleft asunder into two parts  — Bukhari 4868
Narrated `Abdullah bin Masud:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to recite: "Fahal-min-Maddakir (then is there any that will receive admonition?)  — Bukhari 4869
Narrated `Abdullah bin Masud: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to recite: "Fahal-min-Maddakir (then is there any that will receive admonition?)  — Bukhari 4872
Narrated `Abdullah:I recited before the Prophet (ﷺ) 'Fahal-min-Mudhdhakir'. The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "It is Fahal-min Muddakir  — Bukhari 4874
Narrated Sa`id:I asked Ibn `Abbas about Surat Al-Hashr. He replied, "Say Surat An-Nadir  — Bukhari 4883
Narrated `Abdullah (bin Mas'ud):Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) has cursed the lady who uses false hair  — Bukhari 4887
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said. Then some men from these people would attain it  — Bukhari 4898
Narrated `Abdullah:(Similarly--as no. 452 above)  — Bukhari 4931
Narrated Abu `Abdur-Rahman:`Ali said, "We were sitting with the Prophet," (He then mentioned the Hadith above)  — Bukhari 4945.2
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) returned to Khadija and said, "Wrap me! Wrap me!" (Then the sub-narrator narrated the rest of the narration)  — Bukhari 4957
Narrated Al-Bara':I learnt, 'Glorify the Name of your Lord the Most High' (Surat al-A'la) No 87, before the Prophet (ﷺ) came (to Medina)  — Bukhari 4995
Narrated `Uthman:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The best among you (Muslims) are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it  — Bukhari 5027
Narrated `Uthman bin `Affan:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The most superior among you (Muslims) are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it  — Bukhari 5028
Narrated `Abdullah bin Mughaffal:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) reciting Surat-al-Fath on his she-camel on the day of the Conquest of Mecca  — Bukhari 5034
Narrated Hisham:(The same Hadith as 6.556, adding): which I missed (modifying the Verses)  — Bukhari 5037.2
Narrated Qatada:I asked Anas bin Malik about the recitation of the Prophet. He said, "He used to pray long (certain sounds) very much  — Bukhari 5045
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr:The Prophet (ﷺ) asked me, "How long does it take you to finish the recitation of the whole Qur'an?  — Bukhari 5053
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to go round (have sexual relations with) all his wives in one night, and he had nine wives  — Bukhari 5068
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) manumitted Safiyya and regarded her manumission as her Mahr  — Bukhari 5086
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Evil omen is in the women, the house and the horse  — Bukhari 5093
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "If at all there is bad omen, it is in the horse, the woman, and the house  — Bukhari 5095
Narrated Usama bin Zaid:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "After me I have not left any trial more severe to men than women  — Bukhari 5096
Narrated Jabir:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade that a woman should be married to man along with her paternal or maternal aunt  — Bukhari 5108
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) got married while he was in the state of Ihram  — Bukhari 5114
Narrated `Ali:I said to Ibn `Abbas, "During the battle of Khaibar the Prophet (ﷺ) forbade (Nikah) Al-Mut'a and the eating of donkey's meat  — Bukhari 5115
Narrated `Aisha:I said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! A virgin feels shy." He said, "Her consent is (expressed by) her silence  — Bukhari 5137
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to a man, "Marry, even with (a Mahr equal to) an iron ring  — Bukhari 5150
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) consummated his marriage with a woman (Zainab), so he sent me to invite men to the meals  — Bukhari 5170
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Set the captives free, accept the invitation (to a wedding banquet), and visit the patients  — Bukhari 5174
Narrated Jabir:We used to practice coitus interruptus during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Bukhari 5207
Narrated Salim's father:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If the wife of anyone of you asks permission to go to the mosque, he should not forbid her  — Bukhari 5238
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) disliked that one should go to one's family at night (on returning from a journey)  — Bukhari 5243
Narrated Ibn `Umar:(Divorcing my wife during her menses) was counted as one legal divorce  — Bukhari 5253
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:similarly as above  — Bukhari 5257
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I saw him as a slave, (namely, Barira's husband)  — Bukhari 5280
Narrated Ibn `Umar:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "Afflictions will emerge from here," pointing towards the East  — Bukhari 5296
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) made an Ansari man and his wife carry out Lian, and then separated them by divorce  — Bukhari 5314
Narrated 'Urwa:Aisha disapproved of what Fatima used to say  — Bukhari 5327
Narrated Al-Hasan:Ma'qil gave his sister in marriage and later her husband divorced her once  — Bukhari 5330
Narrated Um 'Atiyya:We were forbidden to mourn for more than three days except for a husband  — Bukhari 5340
Narrated Abu Mas`ud:The Prophet (ﷺ) prohibited taking the price of a dog, the earnings of a soothsayer and the money earned by prostitution  — Bukhari 5346
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade taking the earnings of a slave girl by prostitution  — Bukhari 5348
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Allah said, 'O son of Adam! Spend, and I shall spend on you  — Bukhari 5352
Narrated Abu Huraira: The family of Muhammad did not eat their fill for three successive days till he died  — Bukhari 5374
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) died when we had satisfied our hunger with the two black things, i.e. dates and water  — Bukhari 5383
Narrated Abu Juhaifa:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "I do not take my meals while leaning (against something)  — Bukhari 5398
Narrated Abu Juhaifa:While I was with the Prophet (ﷺ) he said to a man who was with him, "I do not take my meals while leaning  — Bukhari 5399
Narrated Abu Qatada:We went out towards Mecca with the Prophet  — Bukhari 5406
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The superiority of `Aisha to other women is like the superiority of Tharid to other kinds of food  — Bukhari 5419
Narrated Jabir:We used to carry the meat of the Hadis (sacrificed animals) to Medina during the life-time of the Prophet  — Bukhari 5424
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The superiority of `Aisha to other ladies is like the superiority of Tharid to other kinds of food  — Bukhari 5428
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to love sweet edible things and honey  — Bukhari 5431
Narrated `Abdullah bin Ja`far bin Abi Talib:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) eating fresh dates with snake cucumber  — Bukhari 5440
Aishah (ra) said, "When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) died, we had been satisfied by the two black things, i.e., dates and water  — Bukhari 5442
Narrated `Abdullah bin Ja`far:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) eating fresh dates with snake cucumbers  — Bukhari 5447
Narrated `Abdullah bin Ja`far:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) eating fresh dates with snake cucumbers  — Bukhari 5449
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, 'When you eat, do not wipe your hands till you have licked it, or had it licked by somebody else  — Bukhari 5456
Narrated Nafi: Once Ibn Umar was taking his supper while he was listening to the recitation of (Quran by) the Imam (in the Isha prayer)  — Bukhari 5464
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If the Iqama for (`Isha') prayer is proclaimed and supper is served, take your supper first  — Bukhari 5465
Narrated Salman bin 'Amri Ad-Dabbi, the Prophet (ﷺ) said, 'Aqiqa is to be offered for a (newly born) boy  — Bukhari 5471
Narrated Abu Qatada:(the same Hadith above, but he added); The Prophet (ﷺ) asked, "Is there any of its meat left with you?  — Bukhari 5491
Narrated Ibn Abi `Aufa:We participated with the Prophet (ﷺ) in six or seven Ghazawat, and we used to eat locusts with him  — Bukhari 5495
Narrated Ka`b bin Malik:A lady slaughtered a sheep with a stone and then the Prophet (ﷺ) was asked about it and he permitted it to be eaten  — Bukhari 5504
Narrated Asma bint Abu Bakr:We slaughtered a horse (by Nahr) during the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) and ate it  — Bukhari 5510
Narrated Asma':We slaughtered a horse (by Dhabh) during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) while we were at Medina, and we ate it  — Bukhari 5511
Narrated Asma' bint Abu Bakr:We slaughtered a horse (by Nahr) during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and ate it  — Bukhari 5512
Narrated `Abdullah bin Yazid:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade An-Nuhba and Al-Muthla  — Bukhari 5516
Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) eating chicken  — Bukhari 5517
Narrated Asma':We slaughtered a horse during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and ate it  — Bukhari 5519
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) made the meat of donkeys unlawful on the day of the battle of Khaibar  — Bukhari 5521
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) prohibited the eating of donkey's meat  — Bukhari 5522
Narrated `Ali:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited Al-Mut'a marriage and the eating of donkey's meat in the year of the Khaibar battle  — Bukhari 5523
Narrated Al-Bara' and Ibn Abi `Aufa:The Prophet (ﷺ) prohibited the eating of donkey's meat  — Bukhari 5525
Narrated Abu Tha`laba:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the eating of the meat of beasts having fangs  — Bukhari 5530
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) passed by a dead goat and said, "There is no harm if its owners benefit from its skin  — Bukhari 5532
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "I do not eat mastigure, but I do not prohibit its eating  — Bukhari 5536
Ibn 'Umar said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to slaughter (camels and sheep, etc.,) as sacrifices at the Musalla  — Bukhari 5552
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to offer two rams as sacrifices, and I also used to offer two rams  — Bukhari 5553
Narrated Anas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came towards two horned rams having black and white colors and slaughtered them with his own hands  — Bukhari 5554
Narrated Ibn `Umar:"Alcoholic drinks were prohibited (by Allah) when there was nothing of it (special kind of wine) in Medina  — Bukhari 5579
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Alcoholic drinks were prohibited. At that time these drinks used to be prepared from unripe and ripe dates  — Bukhari 5584
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about Al-Bit. He said, "All drinks that intoxicate are unlawful (to drink)  — Bukhari 5585
Narrated 'Umar: "Alcoholic drinks are prepared from five things, i.e., raisins, dates. wheat, barley and honey  — Bukhari 5589
Narrated `Ali:the Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the use of Ad-Dubba' and Al Muzaffat. A'mash also narrated this  — Bukhari 5594
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to like sweet edible things and honey  — Bukhari 5599
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the drinking of alcoholic drinks prepared from raisins, dates, unripe dates and fresh ripe dates  — Bukhari 5601
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) drank milk and then rinsed his mouth and said, "It contains fat  — Bukhari 5609
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to like sweet edible things (syrup, etc.) and honey  — Bukhari 5614
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) drank Zamzam (water) while standing  — Bukhari 5617
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the bending of the mouths of water skins for the sake of drinking from them  — Bukhari 5625
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the drinking of water directly from the mouth of a water skin  — Bukhari 5628
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the drinking of water direct from the mouth of a water-skin  — Bukhari 5629
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "If Allah wants to do good to somebody, He afflicts him with trials  — Bukhari 5645
Narrated Aisha:I never saw anybody suffering so much from sickness as Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Bukhari 5646
Narrated Abu Muisa Al-Ash`ari:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and set free the captives  — Bukhari 5649
Narrated Jabir:The Prophet (ﷺ) came to visit me (while I was sick) and he was riding neither a mule, nor a horse  — Bukhari 5664
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There is no disease that Allah has created, except that He also has created its treatment  — Bukhari 5678
Narrated Abu Huraira:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying, "There is healing in black cumin for all diseases except death  — Bukhari 5688
Narrated Hisham's father:`Aisha used to recommend at-Talbina and used to say, "It is disliked (by the patient) although it is beneficial  — Bukhari 5690
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) was cupped while he was in a state of Ihram  — Bukhari 5695
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Fever is from the heat of Hell, so abate fever with water  — Bukhari 5725
Narrated Rafi` bin Khadij:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying, "Fever is from the heat of Hell, so abate fever with water  — Bukhari 5726
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Neither Messiah (Ad-Dajjal) nor plague will enter Medina  — Bukhari 5731
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "(Death from) plague is martyrdom for every Muslim  — Bukhari 5732
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) ordered me or somebody else to do Ruqya (if there was danger) from an evil eye  — Bukhari 5738
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The effect of an evil eye is a fact." And he prohibited tattooing  — Bukhari 5740
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There is no 'Adwa, nor Tiyara, nor Hama, nor Safar  — Bukhari 5757
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Avoid the Mubiqat, i.e., shirk and witchcraft  — Bukhari 5764
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No 'Adwa nor Tiyara; but I like Fal." They said, "What is the Fal?" He said, "A good word  — Bukhari 5776
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The part of an Izar which hangs below the ankles is in the Fire  — Bukhari 5787
Narrated Mu'tamir:I heard my father saying, "I saw Anas wearing a yellow hooded cloak of Khazz  — Bukhari 5802
Narrated Anas bin Malik:In the year of the conquest of Mecca the Prophet (ﷺ) entered Mecca, wearing a helmet on his head  — Bukhari 5808
Narrated Qatada:I asked Anas, "What kind of clothes was most beloved to the Prophet?" He replied, "The Hibra (a kind of Yemenese cloth)  — Bukhari 5812
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The most beloved garment to the Prophet (ﷺ) to wear was the Hibra (a kind of Yemenese cloth)  — Bukhari 5813
Narrated `Aisha:(the wife of the Prophet) When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) died, he was covered with a Hibra Burd (green square decorated garment)  — Bukhari 5814
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, Whoever wears silk in this world shall not wear it in the Hereafter  — Bukhari 5832
Narrated Ibn Azib:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade us to use the red Mayathir and to use Al-Qassiy  — Bukhari 5838
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed Az-Zubair and `Abdur-Rahman to wear silk because they were suffering from an itch  — Bukhari 5839
Narrated Anas bin Malik:that he had seen Um Kulthum, the daughter of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) , wearing a red silk garment  — Bukhari 5842
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade men to use saffron  — Bukhari 5846
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade Muhrims to wear clothes dyed with Wars or saffron  — Bukhari 5847
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) was of a modest height. I saw him wearing a red suit, and I did not see anything better than him  — Bukhari 5848
Narrated Sa`id Abu Maslama:I asked Anas (bin Malik), "Did the Prophet (ﷺ) use to offer the prayers with his shoes on?" He said, "Yes  — Bukhari 5850
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to like starting from the right in performing ablution, combing his hair and putting on his shoes  — Bukhari 5854
Narrated Anas:The sandal of the Prophet (ﷺ) had two straps  — Bukhari 5857
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) called for the Ansar and gathered them in a leather tent  — Bukhari 5860
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the wearing of a gold ring  — Bukhari 5864
Narrated Anas:The ring of the Prophet (ﷺ) was of silver, and its stone was of silver too  — Bukhari 5870
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "To get the moustaches cut 'short is characteristic of the Fitra  — Bukhari 5888
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Cut the moustaches short and leave the beard (as it is)  — Bukhari 5893
Narrated `Uthman bin `Abdullah bin Mauhab:I went to Um Salama and she brought out for us some of the dyed hair of the Prophet  — Bukhari 5897
Ibn Mauhab also said that Um Salama had shown him the red hair of the Prophet  — Bukhari 5898
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Jews and Christians do not dye their hair so you should do the opposite of what they do  — Bukhari 5899
Narrated Anas : The hair of the Prophet (ﷺ) used to hang down up to his shoulders  — Bukhari 5903
Narrated Anas: The head-hair of the Prophet (ﷺ) used to hang down to his shoulders  — Bukhari 5904
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) had big feet and a good-looking face and I have not seen anybody like him after him  — Bukhari 5908
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) had big feet and hands  — Bukhari 5910
Narrated Anas or Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) had big hands and feet and I have not seen anybody like him after him  — Bukhari 5911
Narrated `Aisha:As if I am now looking at the shine of the hair parting of the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was in the state of Ihram  — Bukhari 5918
Narrated (Abdullah) bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade Al-Qaza' (leaving a tuft of hair here and there after shaving one's head)  — Bukhari 5921
Narrated `Aisha:I used to comb the hair of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) during my periods. This hadith also reaches us through another chain  — Bukhari 5925
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to like to start from the right side as far as possible in combing and in performing ablution  — Bukhari 5926
Narrated `Aisha:used to perfume the Prophet (ﷺ) before his assuming the state of with the best scent available  — Bukhari 5928
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The evil eye is a fact," and he forbade tattooing  — Bukhari 5944
Narrated Abu Talha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Angels do not enter a house in which there is a dog or there are pictures  — Bukhari 5949
Narrated `Aisha:I never used to leave in the Prophet (ﷺ) house anything carrying images or crosses but he obliterated it  — Bukhari 5952
Aisha added:The Prophet (ﷺ) and I used to take a bath from one container (of water)  — Bukhari 5956
Narrated `Abbad bin Tamim's uncle:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) lying-down in the mosque and placing one leg on the other  — Bukhari 5969
Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im:That he heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "The person who severs the bond of kinship will not enter Paradise  — Bukhari 5984
Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who is not merciful to others, will not be treated mercifully  — Bukhari 6013
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, Enjoining, all that is good is a Sadaqa  — Bukhari 6021
Narrated Jabir:Never was the Prophet (ﷺ) asked for a thing to be given for which his answer was 'no  — Bukhari 6034
Narrated `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Abusing a Muslim is Fusuq (i.e., an evil-doing), and killing him is Kufr (disbelief)  — Bukhari 6044
Narrated Abu Usaid As-Sa`idi:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The best family among the Ansar is the Banu An-Najjar  — Bukhari 6053
Narrated Hudhaifa:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "A Qattat will not enter Paradise  — Bukhari 6056
Narrated `Aisha:I never saw the Prophet (ﷺ) laughing to an extent that one could see his palate, but he always used to smile only  — Bukhari 6092
Narrated Tariq:`Abdullah said, "The best talk is Allah's Book (Qur'an), and the best guidance is the guidance of Muhammad  — Bukhari 6098
Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Umar: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, 'If anyone says to his brother, 'O misbeliever! Then surely, one of them such  — Bukhari 6104
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A believer is not stung twice (by something) out of one and the same hole  — Bukhari 6133
Narrated Ubai bin Ka`b:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Some poetry contains wisdom  — Bukhari 6145
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to Hassan, "Lampoon them (the pagans) in verse, and Gabriel is with you  — Bukhari 6153
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "It is better for a man to fill the inside of his body with pus than to fill it with poetry  — Bukhari 6154
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Everyone will be with those whom he loves  — Bukhari 6168
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "None of you should say Khabuthat Nafsi, but he is recommended to say 'Laqisat Nafsi  — Bukhari 6179
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "They say Al-Karm (the generous), and in fact Al-Karm is the heart of a believer  — Bukhari 6183
Narrated Abu Huraira:Abu-l-Qasim (The Prophet) said, "Name yourselves by my name, but do not call yourselves by my Kuniya  — Bukhari 6188
Narrated Al-Bara:When Ibrahim (the son of the Prophet) died, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There is a wet nurse for him in Paradise  — Bukhari 6195
Narrated Al-Mughira bin Shuba:Solar eclipse occurred on the day of Ibrahim's death (the Prophet's son)  — Bukhari 6199
Narrated Anas bin Malik:that he passed by a group of boys and greeted them and said, "The Prophet (ﷺ) used to do so  — Bukhari 6247
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (in the above narration No. 268), "And then raise your head till you feel at ease while sitting  — Bukhari 6252
Narrated Qatada:I asked Anas, "Was it a custom of the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) to shake hands with one another?" He said, "Yes  — Bukhari 6263
Narrated `Abdullah bin Hisham:We were in the company of the Prophet (ﷺ) and he was holding the hand of `Umar bin Al-Khattab  — Bukhari 6264
Narrated `Uqba bin Al-Harith:Once the Prophet (ﷺ) offered the `Asr prayer and then he walked quickly and entered his house  — Bukhari 6275
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:We used to have a midday nap and take our meals after the Jumua (prayer)  — Bukhari 6279
Narrated Salim's father:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Do not keep the fire burning in your houses when you go to bed  — Bukhari 6293
Sa'id ibn Jubair said, "Ibn 'Abbas said, 'When the Prophet (ﷺ) died, I had already been circumcised  — Bukhari 6300
Narrated `Aisha:The Verse: 'Neither say your prayer aloud, nor say it in a low tone.' (17.110) was revealed as regards invocation  — Bukhari 6327
Narrated Anas, "The Prophet (ﷺ) raised his hands (in invocation) till I saw the whiteness of his armpits  — Bukhari 6341
Narrated Um Khalid bint Khalid:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) seeking refuge with Allah from the punishment of the grave  — Bukhari 6364
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "There are two blessings that many people are deceived into losing: health and free time  — Bukhari 6412
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) did not eat at a table till he died, and he did not eat a thin nicely baked wheat bread till he died  — Bukhari 6450
Narrated `Aisha:The family of Muhammad did not eat two meals on one day, but one of the two was of dates  — Bukhari 6455
Narrated `Aisha:The bed mattress of the Prophet (ﷺ) was made of a leather case stuffed with palm fibres  — Bukhari 6456
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Give food to the family of Muhammad  — Bukhari 6460
Narrated `Aisha:The most beloved action to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was that whose doer did it continuously and regularly  — Bukhari 6462
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "If you knew that which I know you would laugh little and weep much  — Bukhari 6485
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If you knew that which I know, you would laugh little and weep much  — Bukhari 6486
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The truest poetic verse ever said by a poet, is: Indeed! Everything except Allah, is perishable  — Bukhari 6489
Narrated Anas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "I have been sent and the Hour (is at hand) as these two (fingers)  — Bukhari 6504
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "I have been sent and the Hour (is at hand) as these two (fingers)  — Bukhari 6505
Narrated Abu Qatada:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Relieved or relieving. And a believer is relieved (by death)  — Bukhari 6513
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Do not abuse the dead, for they have reached the result of what they have done  — Bukhari 6516
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "You will meet Allah barefooted, naked, walking on feet, and uncircumcised  — Bukhari 6524
Narrated Sahl:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The people of Paradise will see the Ghuraf (special abodes) in Paradise as you see a star in the sky  — Bukhari 6555
Abu Sa`id added:"As you see a glittering star remaining in the eastern horizon and the western horizon  — Bukhari 6556
Narrated `Abbas:that he said to the Prophet (ﷺ) "Did you benefit Abu Talib with anything?  — Bukhari 6572
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "I am your predecessor at the Lake-Fount  — Bukhari 6575
Narrated Jundab:I heard the Prophet, saying, "I am your predecessor at the Lake-Fount. (Al-Kauthar)  — Bukhari 6589
Narrated `Abdullah:When taking an oath, the Prophet (ﷺ) very often used to say, "No, by Him Who turns the hearts  — Bukhari 6617
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "We (Muslims) are the last in the world, but will be foremost on the Day of Resurrection  — Bukhari 6624
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The oath of the Prophet (ﷺ) used to be: "No, by Him who turns the hearts  — Bukhari 6628
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O followers of Muhammad! By Allah, if you knew what I know, you would weep much and laugh little  — Bukhari 6631
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do not swear by your fathers  — Bukhari 6648
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (ﷺ) ordered us to help others to fulfill the oaths  — Bukhari 6654
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Our (Apostles') property should not be inherited, and whatever we leave, is to be spent in charity  — Bukhari 6727
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The boy is for the owner of the bed  — Bukhari 6750
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The Wala' is for the manumitted (of the slave)  — Bukhari 6752
Narrated `Abdullah:The Muslims did not free slaves as Sa'iba, but the People of the Pre-Islamic Period of Ignorance used to do so  — Bukhari 6753
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the selling of the Wala' (of slaves) or giving it as a present  — Bukhari 6756
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The freed slave belongs to the people who have freed him," or said something similar  — Bukhari 6761
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The son of the sister of some people is from them or from their own selves  — Bukhari 6762
Narrated Usama bin Zaid:the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A Muslim cannot be the heir of a disbeliever, nor can a disbeliever be the heir of a Muslim  — Bukhari 6764
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) beat a drunk with palm-leaf stalks and shoes. And Abu Bakr gave (such a sinner) forty lashes  — Bukhari 6773
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) lashed a drunk with dateleaf stalks and shoes. And Abu Bakr gave a drunk forty lashes  — Bukhari 6776
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The hand should be cut off for stealing something that is worth a quarter of a Dinar or more  — Bukhari 6789
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The hand of a thief should be cut off for stealing a quarter of a Dinar  — Bukhari 6790
