Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The hand should be cut off for stealing a quarter of a Dinar  — Bukhari 6791
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) cut off the hand of a thief for stealing a shield that was worth three Dirhams  — Bukhari 6795
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) cut off the hand of a thief for stealing a shield that was worth three Dirhams  — Bukhari 6796
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) cut off the hand of a thief for stealing a shield that was worth three Dirhams  — Bukhari 6797
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) cutoff the hand of a thief for stealing a shield that was worth three Dirhams  — Bukhari 6798
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever carries arms against us, is not from us  — Bukhari 6874
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) killed a Jew for killing a girl in order to take her ornaments  — Bukhari 6885
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "This and this are the same." He meant the little finger and the thumb  — Bukhari 6895
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I heard the Prophet (saying the same as above Hadith)  — Bukhari 6895.2
Narrated Abu Sa`id:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Do not prefer some prophets to others  — Bukhari 6916
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the practice of An-Najsh  — Bukhari 6963
Narrated Abu Qatada:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A true good dream is from Allah, and a bad dream is from Satan  — Bukhari 6984
Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The (good) dreams of a faithful believer is a part of the forty-six parts of prophetism:  — Bukhari 6987
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The (good) dream of a faithful believer is a part of the forty-six parts of prophetism  — Bukhari 6988
Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying, "A good dream is a part of the forty six parts of prophetism  — Bukhari 6989
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever has seen me in a dream, then no doubt, he has seen me, for Satan cannot imitate my shape  — Bukhari 6994
Narrated Abu Qatada:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever sees me (in a dream) then he indeed has seen the truth  — Bukhari 6996
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The worst lie is that a person claims to have seen a dream which he has not seen  — Bukhari 7043
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whoever takes up arms against us, is not from us  — Bukhari 7070
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever takes up arms against us, is not from us  — Bukhari 7071
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet, said, "Abusing a Muslim is Fusuq (evil doing) and killing him is Kufr (disbelief)  — Bukhari 7076
Narrated Ibn `Umar:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "Do not revert to disbelief after me by striking (cutting) the necks of one another  — Bukhari 7077
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Beware! Do not renegade as (disbelievers) after me by striking (cutting) the necks of one another  — Bukhari 7079
Narrated Ibn 'Umar (ra) :The Prophet (ﷺ) said (about Ad-Dajjal) that he is one eyed, his right eye is as if a protruding out grape  — Bukhari 7123
Narrated `Aisha:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in his prayer, seeking refuge with Allah from the afflictions of Ad-Dajjal  — Bukhari 7129
Narrated Anas:Qais bin Sa`d was to the Prophet (ﷺ) like a chief police officer to an Amir (chief)  — Bukhari 7155
Narrated Abu Musa:that the Prophet (ﷺ) sent him and sent Mu`adh after him (as rulers to Yemen)  — Bukhari 7156
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Set free the captives and accept invitations  — Bukhari 7173
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The most hated person in the sight of Allah, is the most quarrelsome person  — Bukhari 7188
Narrated Anas:If I had not heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "You should not long for death," I would have longed (for it)  — Bukhari 7233
Narrated Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid:Two men sued each other before the Prophet  — Bukhari 7258
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) embraced me and said, "O Allah! Teach him (the knowledge of) the Book (Qur'an)  — Bukhari 7270
Narrated Abal Minhal:Abu Barza said, "(O people!) Allah makes you self-sufficient or has raised you high with Islam and with Muhammad  — Bukhari 7271
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to go to the Quba' mosque, sometimes walking, sometimes riding  — Bukhari 7326
Narrated Ibn `Umar:I heard `Umar (delivering a sermon) on the pulpit of the Prophet  — Bukhari 7337
Narrated As-Sa'ib bin Yazid:That he heard `Uthman bin `Affan delivering a sermon on the pulpit of the Prophet  — Bukhari 7338
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas (ra):The Prophet (ﷺ) sent Mu'adh to Yemen  — Bukhari 7371
Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Allah will not be merciful to those who are not merciful to mankind  — Bukhari 7376
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) frequently used to swear, "No, by the One Who turns the hearts  — Bukhari 7391
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) slaughtered two rams as sacrifice and mentioned Allah's Name and said, "Allahu-Akbar" while slaughtering  — Bukhari 7399
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Do not swear by your fathers; and whoever wants to swear should swear by Allah  — Bukhari 7401
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "When Allah had finished His creation, He wrote over his Throne: 'My Mercy preceded My Anger  — Bukhari 7422
Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "You will definitely see your Lord with your own eyes  — Bukhari 7435
Narrated Al-Mughira:Our Prophet has informed us our Lord's Message that whoever of us is martyred, will go to Paradise  — Bukhari 7530
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to recite the Qur'an with his head in my lap while I used to be in my periods (having menses)  — Bukhari 7549
The same hadith is mentioned through a different chain  — Muslim 96
This hadith is transmitted by Muhammad b. Hatim on the authority of Abu Ayyub Ansari  — Muslim 105
A similar hadith is narrated on Jabir's authority in which the following words are added:I will do nothing more  — Muslim 109
The above hadith has been mentioned with a different chain and slightly different wording  — Muslim 119
The above hadith has been mentioned with a different chain with a slightly different wording at the beginning, then follows the same  — Muslim 122
It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira that the Prophet (ﷺ) said:Iman has over seventy branches, and modesty is a branch of Iman  — Muslim 152
Ishaq b. Ibrahim narrates this hadith of the Prophet on the authority of Imran b. Husain, like the one narrated by Hammad b. Zaid  — Muslim 157
It is narrated on the authority of Jabir that he heard the (Holy Prophet) say:A Muslim is he from whose hand and tongue the Muslims are safe  — Muslim 162
Qutaiba b. Sa'id and Zubair b. Harb say:Jarir narrated this on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of narrators (as mentioned above)  — Muslim 191
Muhammad b. Hatim and others narrate the same hadith of the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) on the authority of Tamim ad-Dari  — Muslim 197
Umayya b. Bistam narrates the same hadith of the Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) on the authority of Tamim ad-Dari  — Muslim 198
Imam Muslim has reported this hadith by Hasan b. 'Ali al-Halwani and other traditions  — Muslim 205
Abu Bakr b. Abu Shaiba narrated a hadith like this from the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) on the authority of Abdullah  — Muslim 222
Abdullah b. Mu'adh narrated from the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) a hadith like this on the authority on Ibn Umar  — Muslim 224
Harmala b. Yahya, Abdullah b. Wahb, Umar b. Muhammad, Ibn Umar narrated like the hadith reported by Shu'ba on the authority of Waqid  — Muslim 226
Jarir b. Abdullah reported it from the Holy Prophet:When the slave runs away from his master, his prayer is not accepted  — Muslim 230
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Tahir with this chain of transmitters  — Muslim 242
A hadith like this as narrated by Ibn 'Umar has also been transmitted by Abu Sa'id al-Khudri  — Muslim 243
Muhammad b. Abu Rafi' narrated the hadith on the authority of Abu Dharr with a slight difference  — Muslim 251
This hadith has also been transmitted on the authority of Sa'd b. Ibrahim with this chain of narrators  — Muslim 264
The same hadith has been narrated by Ishaq b. Mansur on the authority of Jabir with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 271
Iyas b. Salama narrated from his father that the Apostle (ﷺ) observed:He who draws the sword against us is not of us  — Muslim 281
It is narrated on the authority of Abu Musa Ash'ari:He who took up arms against us is not of us  — Muslim 282
This hadith has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of narrators and the transmitters said:He tore and called  — Muslim 286
This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 301
This hadith has been transmitted by Minjab b. al-Harith Tamimi with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 320
The same hadith has been narrated by Zuhair b. Harb, Waki, Ishaq b. Mansur, Husain b. 'Ali  — Muslim 333
The same hadith has been transmitted by Muhammad b. 'Amr, Abu Baker b. Ishaq, Abu'l-Jawwab, A'mash and Abu Huraira  — Muslim 341
This hadith has been narrated by Muhammad b. Hatim, Muhammad b. Bakr, Ahmad b. 'Uthman Naufali, Abu 'Asim, Ibn Juraij  — Muslim 362
It is narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas that he (the Holy Prophet) saw (Allah) with, his heart  — Muslim 436
Abu Bakr b. Abi Shaiba narrated it on the same authorities  — Muslim 438
The same hadith has been by AlMughirah bin Shu’bah attributing it to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 465
Jabir reported that he had heard with his ears the Apostle (ﷺ) saying:Allah will bring out people from the Fire and admit them into Paradise  — Muslim 470
Amr b. Abu Sufyan transmitted a hadith like this from Abu Huraira who narrated it from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 489
This hadith is narrated with the same chain of narrators by Qatada  — Muslim 495
This hadith is narrated by Amr b. Yahya with the same chain of transmitters, but there is no mention of ankles  — Muslim 556
Abdullah, the freed slave of Shahddad, came to 'A'isha and transmitted from her a hadith like this (which she narrated) from the Prophet (ﷺ)  — Muslim 567
In the hadith transmitted by Shu'ba these words are not there:" Complete the Wudu," and there is the name of Abu Yahya al-A'raj (a narrator)  — Muslim 571
Abu Huraira reported:Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) saw a man who did not wash his heel and he remarked: Woe to the heels because of hell-fire  — Muslim 573
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Woe to the heels because of hell-fire  — Muslim 575
A'isha reported:Whenever Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) entered his house, he used tooth-stick first of all  — Muslim 591
Abu Musa reported:I went to the Apostle (ﷺ) and found one end of the tooth-stick upon his tongue (i. e. he was rinsing his mouth)  — Muslim 592
Huddaifa reported:Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) got up for Tahajjud prayer, he cleansed his mouth with the tooth-stick  — Muslim 593
Hudaifa reported:Whenever he (the Holy Prophet) got up for prayer during the night, he cleansed his mouth with the tooth-stick  — Muslim 595
Ibn Umar said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) said: Trim closely the moustache, and let the beard grow  — Muslim 600
Ibn Umar said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) ordered us to trim the moustache closely and spare the beard  — Muslim 601
Ibn Umar said:The Messenger of Allah (may peace be opon him) said: Act against the polytheists, trim closely the moustache and grow beard  — Muslim 602
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Trim closely the moustache, and grow beard, and thus act against the fire-worshippers  — Muslim 603
Jabir reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade the use of bone or the droppings of camels for wiping (after excretion)  — Muslim 608
Ibn Mughira narrated it from his father:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) wiped over his socks and over his forehead and over his turban  — Muslim 634
This hadith has been transmitted by Ibn Mughira on the authority of his father by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 635
This hadith is narrated by Ubaidullah b. 'Amr and Zaid b. Abu Unaisa with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 640
This hadith is transmitted from Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 644
Zahri and Ibn Musayyab have both transmitted a hadith like this from Abu Huraira who narrated it from the Apostle (ﷺ)  — Muslim 645
This hadith has been transmitted by another chain of transmitters in which there is no mention of" throwing away  — Muslim 649
Jabir reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade to urinate in stagnant water  — Muslim 655
Abu Huraira reported:the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: None amongst you should urinate in standing water, and then wash in it  — Muslim 656
Al-Aawad and Hammam reported A'isha as saying:I used to scrape off the (drop of) semen from the garment of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 669
Hammam narrated the hadith from A'isha like the (above-mentioned) traditions  — Muslim 671
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) would recline in my lap when I was menstruating, and recite the Qur'an  — Muslim 693
Ibn 'Abbas reported:The Apostle (ﷺ) woke up at night; relieved himself, and then washed his face and hands and then again slept  — Muslim 698
A'isha reported:Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) had sexual intercourse and intended to eat or sleep, he performed the ablution of prayer  — Muslim 700
Anas reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to have sexual intercourse with his wives with a single bath  — Muslim 708
Ibn Abbas said:Maimuna (the wife of the Holy Prophet) reported to me that she and the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) took a bath from one vessel  — Muslim 733
Ibn Abbas reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) took a bath with the water left over by Maimuna  — Muslim 734
Anas said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) performed ablution with one Mudd and took bath with a Sa' up to five Mudds  — Muslim 737
Maimuna reported:I placed water for the Apostle (ﷺ) and provided privacy for him, and he took a bath  — Muslim 767
Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) observed: Bathing is obligatory in case of seminal emission  — Muslim 776
Abu Ayyub reported that he had heard like this from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 782
Zaid b Thabit reported:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say this: Ablution is obligatory (for one who takes anything) touched by fire  — Muslim 787
Ibn 'Abbas reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) took (meat of) goat's shoulder and offered prayer and did not perform ablution  — Muslim 790
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn 'Abbas on the authority of Maimuna. the wife of the Apostle (ﷺ), by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 796
Ibn Abbas reported:The Apostle (ﷺ) took milk and then called for water and rinsed (his mouth) and said: It contains greasiness  — Muslim 798
This hadith is also narrated by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 803
This hadith is narrated by Ibn Shihab with the same chain of transmitters as transmitted by Yunus  — Muslim 808
Abdullah b. Abbas said:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: When the skin is tanned it becomes purified  — Muslim 812
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 813
A'isha said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) used to remember Allah at all moments  — Muslim 826
Anas reported:Bilal was commanded (by the Holy Prophet) to repeat the phrases twice in Adhan, and once in lqama  — Muslim 841
Ibn Umar reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) had two Mu'adhdhins, Bilal and 'Abdullah b. Umm Maktum, who (latter) was blind  — Muslim 843
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'A'isha by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 844
A'isha reported:Ibn Umm Maktum used to pronounce Adhan at the behest of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) (despite the fact) that he was blind  — Muslim 845
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Hisham  — Muslim 846
Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported:When you hear the call (to prayer), repeat what the Mu'adhdhin pronounces  — Muslim 848
Abu Mu'awiya narrated it on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 855
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: When the Mu'adhdhin calls to prayer, Satan runs back vehemently  — Muslim 857
A hadith like it has been narrated by Abu Huraira but for these words:" He (the man saying the prayer) does not know how much he has prayed  — Muslim 860
It is reported on the authority of Abu Talha that he had heard Anas b. Malik narrating this  — Muslim 893
A hadith like this has been narrated by al-Hakam except that he said:" Bless Muhammad (ﷺ)" and he did not say:" O Allah I  — Muslim 910
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: He who blesses me once, Allah would bless him ten times  — Muslim 912
A hadith like this is narrated by Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 914
This hadith is narrated with the same chain of transmitters by Hisham b. 'Urwa  — Muslim 927
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Hammam b. Munabbih from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 931
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Anas b. Malik by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 946
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 955
This hadith has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 969
This hadith is narrated by Ibn Uyaina with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 973
Anas b. Malik reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Complete the rows, for I can see you behind my back  — Muslim 976
Nu'man b. Bashir reported:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: Straighten your rows, or Allah would create dissension amongst you  — Muslim 978
Abu 'Awana reported this hadith with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 980
This hadith is narrated by Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 986
Ibn 'Umar reported:'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Do not prevent the maid-servants of Allah from going to the mosque  — Muslim 990
A hadith like this has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 993
Abu Huraira said:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Whoever (woman) fumigates herself with perfume should not join us in the 'Isha' prayer  — Muslim 998
This hadith has been narrated by Yahya b. Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1000
A hadith like this has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1003
Abdullah (b. Mas'ud) said:I was not with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) but I wish I were with him  — Muslim 1010
This hadith his been narrated by 'Abu al-Malik with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1017
This hadith is narrated by Jabir b. Samura but with the addition of these words:" (Sa'd said): These bedouins presume to teach me prayer  — Muslim 1019
Amr b. Huwairith reported:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) recite in the morning prayer" Wa'l-lail-i-idhd 'As'asa" (ixxxi)  — Muslim 1023
Abu Barza reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to recite in the morning prayer from sixty to one hundred verses  — Muslim 1031
Abu Barza Aslami reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to recite from sixty to one hundred verses in the morning prayer  — Muslim 1032
Jubair b. Mut'im reported:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) reciting Surat al-Tur (Mountain) (lii) in the evening prayer  — Muslim 1035
This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1036
Al-Bara' b. 'Azib reported that he said prayer with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and he recited:" By the Fig and the Olive  — Muslim 1038
This hadith like one narrated by Hashalm has been narrated from Isma'il with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1045
Uthman b. Abu'l-'As reported:The last thing which the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) instructed me was: When you lead the people in prayer, be brief  — Muslim 1051
Anas reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) used to be brief and perfect in prayer  — Muslim 1052
Anas reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was among those whose prayers was brief and perfect  — Muslim 1053
Anas reported:I never prayed behind an Imam who was more brief and more perfect in prayer than the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1054
Hakam reported:When Matar b. Najiya dominated Kufa he ordered Abu Ubaida to lead people in prayer, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1059
Ali b. Abi Talib reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade me to recite (the Qur'an) in a state of bowing and prostration  — Muslim 1076
Ali b. Abi Talib reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade to recite the Qur'an, while I am in the state of bowing and prostration  — Muslim 1077
Ali reported:My loved one (the Holy Prophet) forbade me that I should recite (the Qur'an) in a state of bowing and prostration  — Muslim 1079
This hadith is transmitted on the authority of 'Ali, but he made no mention of" while in prostration  — Muslim 1081
Ibn 'Abbas reported:I was forbidden to recite (the Qur'an) while I was bowing, and there is no mention of 'Ali in the chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1082
Ibn 'Abbas reported from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ):I was commanded to prostrate myself on seven bones and not to fold back clothing or hair  — Muslim 1096
Ibn 'Abbas reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) had been commanded to prostrate on seven (bones) and forbidden to fold back hair and clothing  — Muslim 1097
Maimuna reported:When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) prostrated himself, if a lamb wanted to pass between his arms, it could pass  — Muslim 1107
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was asked about sutra of a worshipper; he said: Equal to the back of the saddle  — Muslim 1113
Ibn 'Umar said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) used to place his camel (towards the Ka'ba) and said prayer in its direction  — Muslim 1117
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Umar by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1131
This hadith has been narrated from Abu Juhaim Ansari by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1133
This hadith has been transmitted by Humaid b. Hilal on the authority of Yunus  — Muslim 1138
A'isha reported:The Prophet (ﷺ) used to pray at night while I lay interposed between him and the Qibla like a corpse on the bier  — Muslim 1140
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1149
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: None of you must pray in a single garment of which no part comes over his shoulders  — Muslim 1151
Jabir reported:I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) praying in a single garment crossing the two ends  — Muslim 1156
Jabir b. 'Abdullah related that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, and he related like this  — Muslim 1164
Hudhaifa reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said like this  — Muslim 1166
Abu Huraira reported:I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying a hadith like that of Yunus  — Muslim 1169
This hadith has been narratted by Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1170
Anas reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to pray in the folds of the sheep and goats before the mosque was built  — Muslim 1174
Abu al-Tiyyah reported:I heard from Anas a narration like this from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1175
Ibn 'Umar reported:As the people were engaged in the morning prayer a man came to them. The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1179
This hadith has been narrated by Yahya b. Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1200
This hadith has been reported by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1202
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Isma'il b. Abu Khalid  — Muslim 1204
This hadith that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) sent Jabir on an errand has been reported by him through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1208
This hadith has been transmitted by Ibn Abi Shaiba  — Muslim 1210
Mu'aiqib said:They asked the Apostle (ﷺ) about the removal of (pebbles) in prayer, whereupon he said: If you do it, do it only once  — Muslim 1220
Ibn Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saw sputum sticking to the Qibla wall of the mosque, the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1224
Abu Huraira and Abu Sa'id narrated that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saw sputum, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1226
Abu Huraira reported:I perceive as if I am looking at the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) folding up a part of his cloth with another one  — Muslim 1229
Anas b. Malik reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: Spitting in a mosque is a sin, and its expiation is that it should be buried  — Muslim 1231
Sa'd b. Yazid reported:I said to Anas b. Malik: Did the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) pray while putting on the shoes? He said: Yes  — Muslim 1236
Sa'd b. Yazid Abu Mas'ama reported:I said to Anas like (that mentioned above)  — Muslim 1237
Anas b. Malik reported the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying:When the supper is brought and the prayer begins, one, should first take food  — Muslim 1241
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1243
A hadith like this has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) on the authority of Ibn 'Umar with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1245
Abdullah b. 'Atiq narrated from the Apostle (ﷺ) on the authority of 'A'isha, but he made no mention of the account of Qasim  — Muslim 1247
This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1259
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying like this  — Muslim 1261
This hadith has been narrated by al-Zubri with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1266
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters, with a slight modification of words  — Muslim 1275
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters with the words:He should aim at what is correct and complete  — Muslim 1277
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters and said:" He should aim at correctness and that is right  — Muslim 1278
This hadith has been reported by Mansur with the same chain of transwitters and he said:" He should aim at what is according to him correct  — Muslim 1279
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur and he said:" He should aim at correctness  — Muslim 1280
Alqama reported:He (the Holy Prophet) had led them five rak'ahs in prayer  — Muslim 1282
Abdullah b. Mas'ud reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) performed two prostrations for forgetfulness after salutation and talking  — Muslim 1286
A hadith like this has been narrated by AbuSalama on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1300
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Abual-Rahman al-Araj on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1303
Abdullah reported:An Amir or a person pronounced taslim twice. 'Abdullah said: Where did he get this sunnah?  — Muslim 1314
Ibn 'Abbas said:We used to know that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had finished his prayer when we heard the takbir (Allah-O-Akbar)  — Muslim 1316
Abu Huraira reported. I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) seeking refuge from the torment of the grave after this (after the revelation)  — Muslim 1320
A'isha reported:1 heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) seeking refuge from the trial of Dajjal (Antichrist) in prayer  — Muslim 1323
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Ibn Tawus from his father on the authority of AbuHuraira  — Muslim 1329
A hadith like this has been transmitted by A'raj on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1330
Ibn Numair narrated it with the same chain of transmitters and said:O Possessor of Glory and Honour  — Muslim 1336
Warrad, the scribe of Mughira b. Shu'ba, reported:Mu'awiya wrote to Mughira (the contents) of the hadith as transmitted by Mansur and A'mash  — Muslim 1341
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1353
This hadith has been narrated by Shaiban with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1364
Abu Huraira reported the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as saying:He who finds a rak'ah of the prayer, he in fact finds the prayer  — Muslim 1371
Abu Huraira reported the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as saying:He who finds one rak'ah of the prayer with the Imam, he in fact finds the prayer  — Muslim 1372
This hadith is narrated by Abu Huraira with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1375
This hadith has been reported by Ma'mar with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1378
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said the afternoon prayer (at a time) when the (light) of the sun was there in my apartment  — Muslim 1384
Abu Bakr b Abu Shaiban and Yahya b Abu Bukair both of them narrated this hadith with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1387
Abdullah narrated it on the authority of his father Yahya:Knowledge cannot be acquired with sloth  — Muslim 1390
Another hadith like this has been transmitted by Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1396
Abu Huraira reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said:This heat is from the exhalation of Hell-fire, so delay the prayer till it is cool  — Muslim 1398
Jabir b. Samura reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) used to offer the noon prayer when the sun declined  — Muslim 1404
Anas b. Malik reported:We used to offer the 'Asr prayer, then one would go to Quba' and reach there and the sun would be still high  — Muslim 1410
This hadith has been narrated as Marfu by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1418
This hadith has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1421
This hadith has been reported by Yahya b. Abd Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1431
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Angels take turns among you by night and by day, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1433
This hadith has been narrated by the same chain of transmitters by Hammam, and said about Abu Bakr that he was Ibn Abu Musa  — Muslim 1439
This hadith has been narrated by Qurra with the same chain of transmitters, but therein he did not mention:" He turned his face towards us  — Muslim 1450
Jabir b. Samura reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) postponed the last 'Isha' prayer  — Muslim 1453
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira with another chain of transmitters with a very slight change of words  — Muslim 1474
Ibn 'Umar reported from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as some and twenty (degrees)  — Muslim 1480
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1484
This hadith has been narrated by the chain of transmitters by Abu Sahl 'Uthman b. Hakim  — Muslim 1492
This hadith has also been narrated by Shu'ba with this chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1503
Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported that he went to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and found him observing prayer on a mat and prostrating on that  — Muslim 1505
A hadith having the same meaning (as mentioned above) has been transmitted by A'mash  — Muslim 1507
A hadith like this has been narrated by Hammam b. Munabbih on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 1512
This hadith has been transmitted by Taimi with the same chain of narrators  — Muslim 1515
A hadith like this has been narrated by 'Asim with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1517
This hadith has been narrated by Simak with the same chain of transmitters, but no mention has been made of, enough  — Muslim 1527
A hadith like this has been narrated by Qatida with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1530
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Sa'id al-Khudri by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1531
A hadith like this has been narrated by A'mash by the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1533
This hadith has been transmitted by Ayyub with the same chain of narrator  — Muslim 1536
This hadith has been narrated with the same chain of transmitters, but al-Hadra' made this addition:" They both were equal in recitation  — Muslim 1539
This hadith has been narrated by Anas with another chain of transmitters and with minor additions  — Muslim 1551
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Anas from the Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him)  — Muslim 1553
Al-Bari' b. 'Azib reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed Qunut in the morning and evening (prayers)  — Muslim 1555
Al-Bari' reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed Qunut in the dawn and evening (prayers)  — Muslim 1556
This hadith has been narrated by Qatada, but here no mention has been made of" There is no explation for it except this  — Muslim 1567
Ya'la b. Umayya reported:I said to 'Umar b. al-Khattab, and the rest of the hadlth is the same  — Muslim 1574
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Qatada with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1578
A hadith like this has been narrated by Anas by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1587
Yahya b. Abu Ishaq reported:I heard Anas b. Malik say: We went out for Pilgrimage from Medina. The rest is the same  — Muslim 1588
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Anas, but no mention has been made of Pilgrimage  — Muslim 1589
A hadith like this has been narrated by the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1593
A hadith like this has been reported by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1597
Ibn 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to say Nafl prayer on (the back of) his camel in whatever direction it took him  — Muslim 1610
Ibn 'Umar reported that the Apostle (ﷺ) used to pray on (the back of) his camel in whatever direction it took him  — Muslim 1611
Ibn 'Umar reported:I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) praying (Nafl prayer) on a donkey's back while his face was turned towards Khaibar  — Muslim 1614
Abdullah b. 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to observe Witr prayer on his ride  — Muslim 1617
Ibn 'Umar reported:When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was in a state of hurry on a journey, he combined the sunset and 'Isha' prayers  — Muslim 1621
A hadith like this has been narrated by A'mash, with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1639
Anas reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) used to turn to the right (at the end of the prayer)  — Muslim 1641
This hadith has been reported by Mis'ar with the same chain of transmitters, but he made no mention of:" His face would turn towards us  — Muslim 1643
Abu Huraira reported the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as saying:When the prayer commences, there is no prayer but the obligatory one  — Muslim 1645
A hadith like this has been reported by Ishaq with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1646
A hadith like this has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) by Abu Usaid  — Muslim 1653
A hadith like this has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1666
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1673
Abu Huraira reported:My friend Abu'l-Qasim (ﷺ) instructed me to do three things, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 1674
This hadith has been transmitted by Nafi' with the same chain of narrators  — Muslim 1677
Hafsa reported that when it was dawn, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) did not observe (any other prayers) but two short rak'ahs  — Muslim 1678
A hadith like this has been narrated by Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1679
Hafsa reported:When the dawn appeared, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed two rak'ahs (of Sunnah prayers)  — Muslim 1680
A'isha reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to observe two rak'ahs of Sunnah (prayer) when he heard the Adhin and shortened them  — Muslim 1681
This hadith has been narrated by the same chain of transmitters and in the hadith narrated by Usama the words are:" When it was dawn  — Muslim 1682
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger as saying:The two rak'ahs at dawn are better than this world and what it contains  — Muslim 1688
This hadith has been transmitted by another chain of narrators  — Muslim 1693
Abdullah b. Shaqiq reported:I said to 'A'isha and she made a mention of that (recorded above) about the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1709
A'isha reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) died (in this very state) that he observed most of his (Nafl) prayers in a sitting position  — Muslim 1710
A'isha reported:When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) grew bulky and heavy he would observe (most of his Nafl) prayers sitting  — Muslim 1711
Zuhri reported this hadith with the same chain of transmitters, except this that he made a mention of one year or two years  — Muslim 1713
Jabir b. Samura reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed (Nafl) prayer sitting before his death  — Muslim 1714
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Yahya al-A'raj with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1716
A'isha observed that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to observe prayer in the night and the last of his (night) prayer was Witr  — Muslim 1729
A'isha reported:Never did the earlier part of the dawn find the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) but sleeping in my house or near me  — Muslim 1731
A hadith like this has been narrated by 'A'isha by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1733
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed the Witr prayer every night and he completed Witr at the time of dawn  — Muslim 1736
Ibn 'Umar reported the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as say- ing:Hasten to pray Witr before morning  — Muslim 1753
Ibn 'Umar said:He who prayed at night should make Witr the end of his prayer, for the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ordered this  — Muslim 1754
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Make Witr the end of your night prayer  — Muslim 1755
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Witr is a rak'ah at the end of the prayer  — Muslim 1757
Ibn Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Witr is a rak'ah at the end of the night prayer  — Muslim 1758
Jabir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The most excellent prayer is that in which the duration of standing is longer  — Muslim 1768
Ibn `Abbas reported:I spent a night in the house of my mother's sister Maimuna, and the rest of the hadith is the same as narrated above  — Muslim 1802
Abu Jamra reported:I heard Ibn 'Abbas saying that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed thirteen rak'ahs at night  — Muslim 1803
A'isha reported that when the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) stood up at night to pray, he began his prayer with two short rak'ahs  — Muslim 1806
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying When any one of you gets up at night, he should begin the prayer with two short rak'ahs  — Muslim 1807
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Observe some of your prayers in your houses and do not make them graves  — Muslim 1820
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Pray in your houses, and do not make them graves  — Muslim 1821
A hadith like this has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) on the authority of Anas by another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1832
This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters but with a slight modification of words  — Muslim 1850
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of al-Bara' with a slight modification of words  — Muslim 1858
Qatada said:I heard Anas saying that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to Ubayy the same thing  — Muslim 1866
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1879
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Mas'ud from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1881
It is through another chain of transmitters that this hadith has been reported by Abu Mas'ud from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1882
This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters directly from the Companions of Muhammad (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1893
This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1898
This hadith has been transmitted by Zuhri  — Muslim 1901
This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of trans- mitters  — Muslim 1903
This hadith has been narrated by Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1907
This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters with a minor alteration of words  — Muslim 1922
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Basra Ghifari through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1928
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) did not abandon at all observing two rak'ahs after the 'Asr in my house  — Muslim 1935
Abdullah (b. Umar) reported on the authority of his father that he heard like this from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 1954
Sa'id al-Khudri reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Taking a bath on Friday is essential for every adult person  — Muslim 1957
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Juraij with the came chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1962
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 1985
Jabir b. Samura said:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) gave two sermons between which he sat, recited the Qur'an and exhorted the people  — Muslim 1995
Jabir b. Samura reported:I used to pray with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and both his prayer and sermon were of moderate length  — Muslim 2003
This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters on the authority of Husain b. Abd al-Rahman  — Muslim 2017
This hadith is narrated by Jabir through another chain of transmitters but Hammad (one of the narrators) made no mention of the two rak'ahs  — Muslim 2019
This hadith has been narrated by Ibrahim b Muhammad b. al-Muntashir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2029
A hadith like this has been narrated by Sufyan with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2032
Mukhawwil has narrated this hadith on the authority of Sufyan  — Muslim 2033
Salim narrated on the authority of his father that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to observe two rak'ahs after Jumu'a  — Muslim 2041
Ibn 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), Abu Bakr and 'Umar used to observe the two 'Id prayers before the sermon  — Muslim 2052
This hadith has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters but (the narrators) did not make mention of the mosque  — Muslim 2067
Anas b. Malik reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) prayed for rain pointing the back of his hands to the sky  — Muslim 2075
This hadith has been narrated by Anas b. Malik through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2077
Ibn 'Abbas reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:I have been helped by the east wind and the 'Ad were destroyed by the west wind  — Muslim 2087
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2088
Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Exhort to recite" There is no god but Allah" to those of you who are dying  — Muslim 2123
This hadith has been narrated by Sulaiman b. Bilal with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2124
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Exhort to recite" There is no god but Allah" to those of you who are dying  — Muslim 2125
This hadith is narrated on the authority of 'Ala' with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2133
This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters on the authority of 'Asim al-Ahwal  — Muslim 2136
Anas b. Malik reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Endurance is to be shown at the first blow  — Muslim 2139
Umar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:The dead is punished in the grave because of wailing on it  — Muslim 2143
The same hadith is narrated on the authority of 'Umar through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2144
Abdullah b. 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said:The dead is punished because of the lamentation of the living  — Muslim 2152
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn 'Urwa with the same chain of transmitters. The hadith narrated by Abu Usama is more complete  — Muslim 2155
A hadith like this has been narrated by Mughira b. Shu'ba from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2158
This hadith has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2159
Umm 'Atiyya reported:We were forbidden to follow the bier, but it was not made absolute on us  — Muslim 2166
Umm 'Atiyya reported:We were refrained from following the bier, but it was not made absolute on us  — Muslim 2167
Umm 'Atiyya reported:We braided her hair in three plaits  — Muslim 2169
A hadith like this has been narrated by 'Uyaina on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2178
Abu Salama said:I asked 'A'isha with how many garments the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was shourded. She said: With three garments of Sahul  — Muslim 2182
A'isha reported:When the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upom him) died, he was covered with a Yamani wrapper  — Muslim 2183
This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2201
This hadith is narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2206
Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) offered prayer for Ashama, the Negus, and recited four takbirs  — Muslim 2207
The hadith as narrated by Shaibani has been narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2213
Anas reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) observed prayer on the grave  — Muslim 2214
Ibn Juraij told me that Abu Zubair heard Jabir say that the Prophet (ﷺ) kept standing for a bier until it disappeared  — Muslim 2223
Again Abu Zubair heard Jabir say that the Prophet (ﷺ) and his Companions kept standing for a bier of a Jew until it disappeared from sight  — Muslim 2224
A hadith like this has been narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2234
This hadith has been narrated by Husain with the same chain of transmitters, but no mention is made of Umm Ka'b  — Muslim 2236
Ibn 'Abbas said that a piece of red stuff was put in the grave of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2241
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Jabir b. 'Abdullah  — Muslim 2246
Jabir said that he was forbidden to plaster graves  — Muslim 2247
A hadith like this has been narrated by Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2249
Abu Marthad al-Ghanawi reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not sit on the graves and do not pray facing towards them  — Muslim 2250
Abu Marthad al-Ghanawi reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not pray facing towards the graves, and do not sit on them  — Muslim 2251
A hadith like this has been narrated by 'Amr b. Yahya with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2264
Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:No Sadaqa is payable on less than five wasqs of dates or grains  — Muslim 2267
A hadith like this has been narrated by Isma'il b. Umayya with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2269
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messsenger (ﷺ) as saying:No Sadaqa is due from a Muslim on his slave or horse  — Muslim 2273
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:No Sadaqa is due from a Muslim on his slave or horse  — Muslim 2274
A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2275
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no Sadaqa on a slave except Sadaqat-ul-Fitr  — Muslim 2276
Abu Sa'id al Khudri reported:We used to take out the Zakat of Fitr in three kinds, cheese, dates and barley  — Muslim 2286
Ibn 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ordered that the Sadaqat-ul-Fitr should be paid before the people go out for prayer  — Muslim 2288
Abdullah b 'Umar reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ordered the payment of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr before people go out for prayer  — Muslim 2289
This hadith has been narrated by Muhammad b. Isma'il with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2299
Abu Huraira reported the same from the Prophet (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2303
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn 'Urwa with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2321
Hudhaifa and Abu Shaiba reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Every act of goodness is sadaqa  — Muslim 2328
Narrated Abu Huraira:This hadith has been transmitted through another chain the same as the narration of Ya'qub from Suhail  — Muslim 2345
This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters (with this alteration of words):" from the food of her husband  — Muslim 2365
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Asma' through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2377
This hadith has been narrated with the same chain of transmitters except with this change (of words):" Which charity is most excellent?  — Muslim 2384
This hadith has been narrated by Abdullah b. al-Sa'di from 'Umar b. al-Khattab who heard it from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2407
A hadith like this has been narrated by Anas through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2413
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2414
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:O Allah, make the provision of Muhammad's family sufficient just to sustain life  — Muslim 2427
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2434
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas b. Malik through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2438
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jabir b. 'Abdullah through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2450
Abida said:I will not narrate to you except what I heard from him (Hadrat 'Ali), and then he narrated from him  — Muslim 2466
Sahl b. Hunaif reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:There would arise from the east a people with shaven heads  — Muslim 2472
This very hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sbu'ba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2475
Anas b. Malik reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) found a date in the street and said:If it were not of sadaqa I would have eaten it  — Muslim 2478
This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of trainsmitters  — Muslim 2484
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'isha through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2488
Jarir b. 'Abdullah said:'When the collector of sadaqat (Zakat) comes to you, (you should see) that he goes away pleased with you  — Muslim 2494
Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with both of them) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The month may consist of twenty-nine days  — Muslim 2507
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yahya b. Abi Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2519
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Juraij with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2524
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Abu Khalid with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2527
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of 'A'isha (Allah be pleased with her)  — Muslim 2539
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah on the two chains of transmitters  — Muslim 2540
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Samura b. Jundub  — Muslim 2548
Musa b. 'Ali has narrated this hadith through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2551
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Qatada too  — Muslim 2553
A hadith like this has been transmitted by Sahl b. Sa'd  — Muslim 2555
Abu Huraira reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade (his Companions) to observe Saum Wisal  — Muslim 2569
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) said that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to embrace (his wives) while fasting  — Muslim 2578
A hadith like this has been narrated by Yahya b. Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2582
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to kiss her during the month of fasting  — Muslim 2583
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) kissed (his wives) during Ramadan while observing fast  — Muslim 2584
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported that Allah's Apostle (peace be upon him) kissed (his wives) while fasting  — Muslim 2585
Hafsa (Allah be pleased with her) reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) kissed (his wives) while fasting  — Muslim 2586
A hadith like this has been narrated by Hafsa (Allah be pleased with her) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2587
This hadith has been narrated with the same chain of transmitters on the authority of Zuhri  — Muslim 2600
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2627
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Nadr on the authority of Umair, the freed slave of Umm al-Fadl, through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2634
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Abdullah through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2643
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2654
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ayyub with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2659
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah b. Abi Yazid  — Muslim 2663
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) said that the Prophet (ﷺ) forbade to observe fast on two days-the day of Fitr and the day of Adha  — Muslim 2676
Nubaisha al-Hudhali reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The days of Tashriq are the days of eating and drinking  — Muslim 2677
In another version of the previous hadith, the words are:" Yahya said: I think it was due to the regard for the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2689
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas (Allah be pleased with them) from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2695
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sufyan with the same chain of transmitters in which it is said:" Fasting of two months  — Muslim 2700
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Fasting is a shield  — Muslim 2705
This hadith has been narrated by Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2712
The same hadith as the previous hadith has been transmitted through another chain  — Muslim 2727
This hadith is narrated by 'Abdullah b. Hani b. Akhi Mutarrif with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2754
Abu Ayyub al-Ansari reported (through another chain of transmitters):I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying like this  — Muslim 2759
Abu Ayyub reported a hadith like this (through another chain of transmitters)  — Muslim 2760
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Seek Lailat-ul-Qadr in the last week (of Ramadan)  — Muslim 2762
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to observe i'tikaf in the last ten days of Ramadan  — Muslim 2782
This hadith has been narrated by 'A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2783
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported:I never saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) fasting in the ten days of Dhu'I-Hijja  — Muslim 2789
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported:The Apostle of Allah (ﷺ) did not observe fast in the ten days of Dhul-Hijja  — Muslim 2790
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with him) reported:I immediately learnt Talbiya from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), and he then narrated the hadith  — Muslim 2813
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) pronounced Talbiya as his camel stood up  — Muslim 2821
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported:I still seem to see; the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 2835
This hadith has been narrated by 'Ubaidullah with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2840
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Ayyub  — Muslim 2878
Ibn 'Abbas (Allah be pleased with them) reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) got himself cupped in the state of lhrim  — Muslim 2885
Ibn Buhaina reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) got himself cupped in the middle of his head on his way to Mecca  — Muslim 2886
This hadith has been reported on the authority of A'isha through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2904
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 2907
A'Isha reported that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) entered into the state of Ihram for Hajj Afrad  — Muslim 2921
AI-Qasim b. Muhammad reported that A'isha had come for Hajj  — Muslim 2924

Abu Dharr (Allah be pleased with him) said that Tamattu' in Hajj was a special (concession) only for the Companions of Muhammad (ﷺ)  — Muslim 2965
Abu Dharr (Allah be pleased with him) reported:Tamattu' in Hajj was a special concession for us  — Muslim 2966
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Sulaiman (but with a slight modification of words)  — Muslim 2971
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mutarrif with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 2975

This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 3006
This hadith is narrated by Salim b. Hayyin with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 3027
Jabir b." Abdullah (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) walked swiftly in three circuitsfrom stone to stone  — Muslim 3054
Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) and his Companions did not observe Sa'i between al-Safa' and al-Marwa but only one Sa'i  — Muslim 3085
Ibn Juraij reported on the same authority a hadith like that, and said:But one Tawaf and that was the first Tawaf  — Muslim 3086
Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) observed the sunset and 'Isha' prayers together at Muzdalifa  — Muslim 3110
This hadith has been transmitted by Al-A`mash with a slight variation of words, i.e. he said before its time when it was still dark  — Muslim 3117
A hadith like this has been narrated by 'Abd al-Rahman b. al-Qasim with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3121

A'mash reported:I heard Hajjaj saying I Do not say Surah al-Baqara. The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 3133

Jabir b. Abdullah reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ)  — Muslim 3142

Ibn 'Umar reported:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying like this  — Muslim 3381
Ibn Umar narrated from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) a hadlth like this  — Muslim 3382
Ibn Umar reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) visited (the mosque) at Quba' riding and on foot  — Muslim 3389
Ibn 'Umar reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came to Quba' riding as well as on foot  — Muslim 3391
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3392
Abdullah b. Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to come to Quba' riding and on foot  — Muslim 3393
Ibn 'Umar had narrated this hadith through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3394
Ibn Umar used to come to Quba' on every Saturday and he said:I saw Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) coming (to this place) on every Saturday  — Muslim 3395
This hadlth has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Dinar, but he made no mention of:" Every Saturday  — Muslim 3397
Salama b. al. Akwa' and Jabir b. Abdullah reported:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came to us and permitted us to contract temporary marriage  — Muslim 3414
Rabi' b. Sabra reported on the authority of his father that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) prohibited the contracting of temporary marriage  — Muslim 3426
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Amr b. Dinar  — Muslim 3445
Ibn 'Abbas (Allah be pleased with them) reported:Allah's Messenger. (ﷺ) married Maimuna while he was a Muhrim  — Muslim 3452

Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited Shighar  — Muslim 3467
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as having said:There is no Shighar in islam  — Muslim 3468
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Vmar with the same chain of transmitters, but there is no mention of Ibn Numair  — Muslim 3470
Jabir b. Abdullah (Allah be pleased with him) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited Shighar  — Muslim 3471
Anas b. Malik reported that 'Abd al-Rahman married a woman for a datestone weight of gold  — Muslim 3495
Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with him) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said:When any one of you is invited to a feast, he should attend it  — Muslim 3509
Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messenger (may be upon him) having said this:Accept the feast, when you are invited  — Muslim 3512
Abdullah b. Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messen- ger (ﷺ) as saying:Come to the feast, when you are invited  — Muslim 3515

The above hadith has likewise been narrated through another chain  — Muslim 3683
A'isha reported:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave us the option (to get divorce) but we did not deem it as divorce  — Muslim 3684
A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave option to his wives, but it was not a divorce  — Muslim 3686

A'isha reported that the husband of Barira was a slave  — Muslim 3785
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3800
Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade (two types of transactions) Mulamasa and Munabadha  — Muslim 3801

Abu Huraira reported from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) a hadith like this through another chain cf transmitters  — Muslim 3803
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3807
Abdullah (b. 'Umar) (Allah be pleased with him) said that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the transaction called habal al-habala  — Muslim 3809
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:A Muslim should not purchase (in opposition) to his brother  — Muslim 3813
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through another chain of transmitters but with a slight change of words  — Muslim 3814
Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the outbidding (against another)  — Muslim 3818
This hadith has been reported on the authority of 'Ubaidullah  — Muslim 3820
Abdullah (Allah be pleased with him) reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Do not meet the traders (in the way)  — Muslim 3821
Jabir (Allah be pleased with him) reported a similar hadith from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3827
Anas b. Malik (Allah be pleased with him) said:We were forbidden that a townsman should sell for a man of the desert  — Muslim 3829
Ayyub narrated with the same chain of transmitters but with this change of words:" He who buys a goat has the option  — Muslim 3834
A hadith like this has been narrated through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3838
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3854
Another chain on the authority of Ibn 'Umar narrated the same as the above hadith  — Muslim 3863
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Umar through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3867
Nafi, reported on the authority of Ibn Umar (Allah be pleased with them) a hadith like that narrated before  — Muslim 3868
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Nafi' with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3881
Ubaidullah reported this hadith with a slight change of words on the same authority (as quoted above)  — Muslim 3885
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Sahl b. Abu Hathma  — Muslim 3890
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3895
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Ayyub  — Muslim 3898
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Nafi with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3900
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Nafi, with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3904
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of al Zuhri  — Muslim 3906
Ibn Umar reported on the authority of his father as Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) saying so  — Muslim 3907
Jabir b. 'Abdullah (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the types of sales as described before  — Muslim 3909
Jabir b. Abdullah (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having forbidden the renting of land  — Muslim 3916
Jabir b. Abdullah (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having forbidden taking of rent or share of land  — Muslim 3919
Jabir (Allah be pleased with him) reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) having forbidden Mukhabara  — Muslim 3922
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight change of words  — Muslim 3927
Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbidding Muhaqala and Muzabana  — Muslim 3933
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported:Rafi forbade us from benefitting from our land (in the form of rent)  — Muslim 3937
Nafi, reported from Ibn Umar (Allah be pleated with them) that he came to Rafi and he narrated this hadith from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ)  — Muslim 3941
Another chain narrated the same as the above hadith  — Muslim 3947
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Rafi from the Prophet (ﷺ) about this, but he did not make mention of his uncle Zuhair  — Muslim 3950
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yahya b. Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3954
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Abbas (Allah be pleased with them)  — Muslim 3959
This hadith is transmitted on the authority of Abu Muawiya (but With a slight change of words)  — Muslim 3972
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Juraij with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3976
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Bukair b. al-Ashajj with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 3982
A hadith like this is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him)  — Muslim 3999
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ayyob with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4001
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him)  — Muslim 4003
Jabir b. 'Abdullah (, Allah be pleased with him) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the sale of excess water  — Muslim 4004
A hadith like this is reported on the authority of Abu Mas'ud through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4010
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Rafi' b. Khadij through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4013
Abu Zubair said:I asked Jabir about the price of a dog and a cat; he said: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) disapproved of that  — Muslim 4015
Ibn 'Umar (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) giving command for killing dogs  — Muslim 4016
A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 4033
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Yahya b. Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4034
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sufyan b. Abu Zuhair al-Shana'i  — Muslim 4037
Abd al-Rahman b. Wa'ala narrated this on the authority of 'Abdullah b. Abbas  — Muslim 4045
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Amr b. Dinar with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4051
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4056
Uthman b. 'Affan reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not sell a dinar for two dinars and one dirham for two dirhams  — Muslim 4058
The above hadith is likewise narrated through another chain of transmission on the authority of Abi Qilabah  — Muslim 4062
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (Allah be pleased with him) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4065
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Musa b. Abu Tamim with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4070
Abd al-Rabman b. Abu Bakra said:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited us. The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 4074
A hadith like this is narrated on the authority of Sa'id b. Yazid with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4077
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zakariya with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4095
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jabir through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4099
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) reported:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) bought from a Jew grain (as loan) and pledged him his iron coat-of-mail  — Muslim 4115
Ibn Abu Najih has narrated a hadith like this with the same chain of transmitters, but he has not mentioned:" for a definite period  — Muslim 4120
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Abu Najih through another chain of transmitters mentioning in it" for a specified period  — Muslim 4121
Ma'mar b. Abdullah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:No one hoards but the sinner  — Muslim 4123
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Sulaiman b. Bilal from Yahya  — Muslim 4124
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transrmitters  — Muslim 4131
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Salama with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4138
Abu Haraira reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:When you disagree about a path, its breadth should be made seven cubits  — Muslim 4139
This hadith is transmitted on the authority of Shu'ba but with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4149

A hadith like this is reported on the authority of Muhammad son of Fatima (Allah be pleased with her) daughter of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Muslim 4172
Ibn Abbas (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:One who gets back the gift is like one who eats vomit  — Muslim 4174
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with different chains of transmitters and a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4179
Nu'man b. Bashir (Allah be pleased with them) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to his father:Call me not as witness to an injustice  — Muslim 4184

Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported a hadith like this through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4194
Jabir reported this hadith directly from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ)  — Muslim 4195

Uqba. b. Amir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The expiation of the (breach of) a vow is the same as that of the (breach of an oath)  — Muslim 4253
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4258
Abd al-Rahman b. Samura reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not swear by idols, nor by your fathers  — Muslim 4262

Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:An oath is to be interpreted according to the intention of the one who takes it  — Muslim 4284
Abu Huraira reported this hadith from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4287
This hadith is transmitted on the authority of Ibn Umar with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4293
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Umar through another chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4297

This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Ghazwan (and the words are):" I heard Abu'l-Qasim (ﷺ) as the Prophet of repentance  — Muslim 4312

Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) pierced their eyes because they had pierced the eyes of the shepherds  — Muslim 4360
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ya'la  — Muslim 4367

A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) cut off the hand of a thief for a quarter of a dinar rid upwards  — Muslim 4398
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Zuhri  — Muslim 4399
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The hand of a thief should not be cut off but for a quarter of a dinar and upwards  — Muslim 4400

In another version of the tradition, we have the wording:" An Abyssinian slave maimed and disabled  — Muslim 4756
