This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Haiwa with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4984
Abu Tha'laba al-Khushani reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having prohibited the eating of all fanged beasts of prey  — Muslim 4990
This hadith has been narrated through several other chains of transmitters, but some of the chains have a slight variation of words  — Muslim 4991
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The eating of all fanged beasts of prey is unlawful  — Muslim 4992
Ibn 'Abbas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited the eating of all fanged beasts of prey, and all the birds having talons  — Muslim 4994
Ibn Abbas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade (the eating) of all the fanged beasts of prey, and of all the birds having talons  — Muslim 4996
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 4997
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri through a different chain of transmitters with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5006
Abu Tha'laba reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) prohibited (the eating) of the flesh of domestic asses  — Muslim 5007
Ibn Umar reported that Allah's Messenger (way peace be upon him) forbade the eating of the flesh of domestic asses  — Muslim 5008
Bara was heard saying:We were forbidden (to eat) the flesh of the domestic asses  — Muslim 5014
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Asim with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5016
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Yazid b. Abu Ubaid  — Muslim 5019
This hadith has hen transmitted on the authority of Ibn Juraij  — Muslim 5024
Asma' reported:We slaughtered a horse and ate it during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Muslim 5025
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Hisham  — Muslim 5026
Ibn Abu Aufa reported:We went on seven expeditions with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and ate locusts  — Muslim 5045
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Abu Ya'fur with the same chain of transmitters, and he mentioned seven expeditions  — Muslim 5047
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Yahya with a slight change of wording  — Muslim 5049
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Kahmas  — Muslim 5051
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Khalid al-Hadhdha' through different chains o transmitters  — Muslim 5056
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba through other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 5058
Ibn 'Abbas reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said this:Do not make anything having life as a target  — Muslim 5059
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5060
Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade that any beast should be killed after it has been tied  — Muslim 5063
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of al-Aswad b. Qais with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5066
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5068
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of al-Bara' b. 'Azib through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5074
al-Bara' b. 'Azib reported:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) addressed us on the day of Nahr after the ('Id) prayer. The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 5075
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of 'Uqba b. 'Amir al-Juhan with a slight change of wording  — Muslim 5086
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Anas with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5090
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sa'id b. Masruq with the same chain of transmitters with a slight variation of words  — Muslim 5095
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) having said:None of you should eat the flesh of his sacrificial animal beyond three days  — Muslim 5100
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mu'awiya b. Salih with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5111
The above hadith has been narrated likewise through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5130
Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about the use of Khamr from which vinegar is prepared. He said:No (it is prohibited)  — Muslim 5140
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said:Wine is prepared from the (fruit) of these two trees-date-palm and vine  — Muslim 5142
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5143
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Abi Maslama with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5151
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yahya b. Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5155
This hadith is narrated on the authority of Abu Qatada through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5159
The above hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5161
Ibn Umar reported that he was forbidden to prepare Nabidh by mixing unripe dates and fresh dates, and dates with grapes  — Muslim 5164
Ibn 'Umar reported that they were forbidden to prepare Nabidh by mixing dry dates and fresh dates and dates and grapes together  — Muslim 5165
Anas b. Malik reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the preparation of Nabidh in gourd or varnished jar  — Muslim 5166
The above hadith is narrated through a different chain with slight variation in wording  — Muslim 5167
A'isha reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) forbade (the preparation) of Nabidh in gourd and varnished jar  — Muslim 5173
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbidding (the preparation of Nabidh) in varnished jar, green pitcher, gourd, and hollow stump  — Muslim 5176
Ibn 'Abbas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade (preparation) of Nabidh in gourd, in hollow stump and in varnished jar  — Muslim 5181
Abu Sa'id reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade (the preparation) of Nabidh in a green pitcher (besmeared with pitch)  — Muslim 5182
Abu Sa'id reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade drinking in green pitcher, in gourd and in the hollow stump  — Muslim 5185
This hadith is reported on the authority of Jabir b. Abdullah that Nabidh was prepared for him in a big bowl of stone  — Muslim 5205
A'isha reported:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about Bit'i, whereupon he said: Every drink that causes intoxication is forbidden  — Muslim 5211
A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about it, whereupon he said that everything that causes intoxication is forbidden  — Muslim 5212
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Musa b. Uqba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5220
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:He who drank (wine) in this world would be deprived of it in the Hereafter  — Muslim 5222
Ibn 'Umar reported this hadith from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5225
Anas reported:I served drink to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in this cup of mine: honey, Nabidh, water and milk  — Muslim 5237
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jabir b. Abdullah through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5251
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Juraij  — Muslim 5252
Jabir b. Abdullah reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5254
This hadith is reported on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5261
Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said:Do not eat with your left hand, for the Satan eats with his left hand  — Muslim 5264
This hadith is reported by Zuhri on the authority of Sufyan with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5266
Abu Sa'id (Khudri) reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade from turning the waterskins upside down and drinking from its mouth  — Muslim 5271
Anas reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) disapproved the drinking of water while standing  — Muslim 5274
This hadith is reported on the authority of Anas with a different chain of transmitters, but no mention is made of the words of Qatada  — Muslim 5276
Abu Sa'id Khudri reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) warned against drinking while standing  — Muslim 5277
Abu Sa'id Khudri reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters but with a slight, variation of wording  — Muslim 5278
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:None of you should drink while standing; and if anyone forgets, he must vomit  — Muslim 5279
Ibn Abbas reported:I served. (water of) Zamzam to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), and he drank it while standing  — Muslim 5280
Ibn 'Abbas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) drank (water) from Zamzam in a bucket while he was standing  — Muslim 5281
Ibn 'Abbas reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) drank (water) from Zamzam while he was standing  — Muslim 5282
This hadith is reported on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5284
Abu Qatada reported on the authority of his father that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) forbade breathing in a vessel  — Muslim 5285
Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to breathe three times in the course of a drink (i. e. he drank in three gulps)  — Muslim 5286
This hadith is reported on the authority of Anas with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5288
This hadith is reported on the authority of Sahl b. Sa'd with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5293
A hadith like this has been reported on the authority of Ka'b b. Malik through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5299
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sufyan with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5302
Jabir reported from Allah's Messenger. (ﷺ) about mentioning the licking (of fingers) and the (falling of) the mouthful  — Muslim 5305
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Hammad with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5308
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Abu Mas'ud Ansari through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5310
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Jabir also  — Muslim 5311
Abdullah b. Ja'far reported:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) eating cucumber with fresh dates  — Muslim 5330
Anas b. Malik reported:I saw Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) squatting and eating dates  — Muslim 5331
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:A family which has dates will not be hungry  — Muslim 5336
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn Hashim with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5340
Sa'id b. Zaid reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Truffles are 'Manna' and its juice is the medicine for the eyes  — Muslim 5348
A'isha reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:The best of condiments or condiment is vinegar  — Muslim 5350
This Hadith is narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5357
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Mughira with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5363
A hadith like this is reported on the authority of Jabir but with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5369
Jabir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Food for one suffices two and food for two suffices for four  — Muslim 5370
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying that a non-Muslim eats in seven intestines whereas a Muslim eats in one intestine  — Muslim 5372
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar but with a different chain oi transmitters  — Muslim 5373
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:A believer eats in one intestine, whereas a non-believer eats in seven intestines  — Muslim 5375
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Jabir  — Muslim 5376
Abu Musa reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:A believer eats in one intestine, whereas a non-believer eats in seven intestines  — Muslim 5377
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5378
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of A'mash  — Muslim 5382
Umm Salama, the wife of Allah's Apostle (ﷺ), said:He who drinks in the vessel of silver in fact drinks down in his belly the fire of Hell  — Muslim 5385
This hadith has been reported on the authority of `Abdullah b. `Ukaim with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5395
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Hudhaila with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5399
This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Umar through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5402
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5405
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of 'Asim  — Muslim 5412
The previous hadith is narrated likewise through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5414
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Qatada but there is no mention of the words of Abd Uthman  — Muslim 5416
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Muhammad b. Ja'far but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5421
Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said:He who wore silk in this world would not wear it in the Hereafter  — Muslim 5425
Abu Umama reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said:He who wore silk in this world would not wear it in the Hereafter  — Muslim 5426
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of azid b. Abu Habib with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5428
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sa'd with the same chain of transmitters but there is no mention of the word" journey  — Muslim 5430
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Yahya b. Abu Kathir with the same chain of trarnmitteis  — Muslim 5435
Anas reported that the garment most liked by Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was the mantle of Yemen  — Muslim 5441
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ayyub with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5444
A'isha reported that the pillow on which Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) reclined was of leather stuffed with palm fibre  — Muslim 5446
A'isha reported that the bedding on which. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) slept was made of leather stuffed with palm fibre  — Muslim 5447
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Hisham b. 'Urwa with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5448
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sufyan with the saule chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5451
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said:Allah will not look upon him who trails his garment out of pride  — Muslim 5453
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira but with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5466
Abu Huraira reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) forbade the wearing of gold signet ring  — Muslim 5470
The previous hadith is narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5471
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Umar through other chains of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5474
This hadith has also been likewise narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Umar through several other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 5475
A hadith like this has been reported on the authority of Ibn Juraij with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5485
Anas b. Malik reported that the ring of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was made of silver and it had an Abyssinian stone in it  — Muslim 5486
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yunus b. Yazid with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5488
Anas reported that the ring of Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) was on this, and he pointed toward the little finger of his left hand  — Muslim 5489
A hadith like this has been reported on the authority of 'Ali through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5491
Ali b. Abu Talib reported that he (Allah'. s Apostle) forbade or forbade me. the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 5492
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Abu Huraira with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5498
Jalbir b. Abdullah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:None of you should lie on his back and place one of his feet upon the other  — Muslim 5503
Abbad b. Tamim reported from his uncle that he saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) lying in the mosque and placing his one foot upon the other  — Muslim 5504
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitter  — Muslim 5505
Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade that a person should (wear) clothes dyed in saffron  — Muslim 5507
Abu Horaira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The Jews and the Christians do not dye (their hair), so oppose them  — Muslim 5510
Abu Talha reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) having said:Angels do not enter a house in which there is a dog or a picture  — Muslim 5514
Abu Talha reported:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying: Angels do not enter the house in which there is a dog or a statue  — Muslim 5515
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5516
This hadith has been narrpted on the authority of Waki' with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5524
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5527
Ibn 'Umar reported a hadith like this through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5536
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Angels do not accompany the travellers who have with them a dog and a bell  — Muslim 5546
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5547
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The bell is the musical instrument of the Satan  — Muslim 5548
Jabir reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade (the animals to be beaten) on the face or cauterisation on the face  — Muslim 5550
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Jabir b. 'Abdullah through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5551
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Zaid b. Aslam with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5564
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Shu'ba with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5566
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Mansur with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5574
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mansur without the story pertaining to Umm Ya'qub  — Muslim 5575
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abdullah  — Muslim 5576
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Zuhri but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5579
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5585
Ibn Umar reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said:The names dearest to Allah are 'Abdullah and 'Abd al-Rahman  — Muslim 5587
This hadith has been narrated through different chains of transmitters on the authority of Shu'ba with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5594
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn Uyaina, but there is no mention of this:" We will not allow you to cool your eyes  — Muslim 5596
Samura b. Jundub reported AUah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Don't give names to your servants as Rabdh, 'Ya ar, Aflah and Nafi  — Muslim 5600
Ibn 'Umar reported that 'Umar had a daughter who was called 'Asiya. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave her the name of Jamila  — Muslim 5605
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Anas through another chain of transmit ters  — Muslim 5614
A'isha reported that the new-born infants were brought to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ). He blessed them and rubbed their palates with dates  — Muslim 5619
Anas b Malik reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) addressed me:O My Son  — Muslim 5623
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ismail, with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5625
This hadith bu been narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id Khudri through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5630
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Juraij, but there is no mention of the words" business in the market  — Muslim 5632
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Sahl b. Sa'd as-Sa'idi with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5640
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yunus through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5645
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zaid b. Aslam with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5649
Anas b. Malik reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:When the People of the Book offer you salutations, you should say: The same to you  — Muslim 5652
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Umar through another chain of transmitters with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5655
Anas b. Malik reported that when Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) happened to pass by young boys he would great them  — Muslim 5663
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sayyar with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5664
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yazid b. Abu Habib with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5675
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ishaq b. 'Abdullah b. Talha with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5682
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) having said:None of you should make another one stand in the meeting and then occupy his place  — Muslim 5683
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar through another chain of transmitters. but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5685
This hadith hilt been reported on the authority of Ma'mar with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5687
This hadith has been reported on the authority of lbn 'Umar through two different chains of transmitters  — Muslim 5695
This hadith has been reported on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5698
Anas b. Malik reported that a Jewess brought poisoned meat and then served it to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Muslim 5706
This hadith has been reported on the authority of 'A'isha through another chain of transmitters with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5711
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitted  — Muslim 5713
A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) commanded the use of incantation for curing the influence of an evil eye  — Muslim 5720
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mis'ar with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5721
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Juraij with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5728
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of 'Uthman b. Abu al-'As with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5740
Jabir reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) sent a phystian to Ubayy b. Ka'b. He cut the vein and then cauterised it  — Muslim 5745
A'mash reported this with the same chain of transmitters and he made no mention of the fact that he cut one of his veins  — Muslim 5746
Ibn 'Abbas reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) got himself cupped and gave to the cupper his wages and he put the medicine in the nostril  — Muslim 5749
Anas b. Malik reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) got himself cupped and never withheld the wages of anyone  — Muslim 5750
Ibn Umar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Fever is due to vehemence of the heat of Hell, so cool it with water  — Muslim 5752
Ibn Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Fever is from the vehement raging of the fire of Hell, so extinguish it with water  — Muslim 5753
Ibn 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Fever is from the vehement Paging of the Hell-fire, so cool it with water  — Muslim 5754
A'isha reported Allah's messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Fever is from the vehement raging of the Hell-fire, so cool it with water  — Muslim 5755
Rafi' b. Khadij reported:I heard Allah's messenger (ﷺ) as saying: The fever is due to the intense heat of the Hell, so cool it with water  — Muslim 5759
Abu Huraira reported that he heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Nigella seed is a remedy for every disease except death  — Muslim 5766
This hadith has been narrated through other chains of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5767
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no disease for which Nigella seed does not provide remedy  — Muslim 5768
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Juraij through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5776
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5778
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Sa'd b. Malik, Khuzaima b. Thabit and Usama b. Zaid  — Muslim 5781
Ibrahim b. Sa'd b. Abu Waqqas reported:Usama b. Zaid and Sa'd had been sitting and they had been conversing and they said this hadith  — Muslim 5782
This hadith has been transmitted by Ibrahim b. Sa'd b. Malik on the authority of his father  — Muslim 5783
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of 'Abdullah b. Harith with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5786
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no transitive disease, no huma, no star promising rain, no safar  — Muslim 5794
Jabir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no transitive disease, no ghoul, no safar  — Muslim 5796
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no transitive disease, no divination, but I like good words  — Muslim 5802
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There is no transitive disease, no hama, no divination, but I like good omen  — Muslim 5803
Abdullah b. 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:If there be bad luck, it is in the house, and the wife, and the horse  — Muslim 5804
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Zuhri with other chains of transmitters but with slight variations of wording  — Muslim 5806
Sahl b. Sa'd reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:If bad luck were to be in anything, it is found in the woman, the horse and the abode  — Muslim 5810
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sahl b. Sa'd with a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5811
Jabir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:If bad luck were to be in anything, it is found in the land, in the servant and in the horse  — Muslim 5812
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Zuhri with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5814
The hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri through the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5820
Nafi' reported that he heard Abu Lubaba informing Ibn 'Umar that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had forbidden the killing of domestic snakes  — Muslim 5830
Abdullah reported that Abu Lubaba had informed him that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had forbidden the killing of the snakes found in the house  — Muslim 5831
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of al-A'mash with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5836
Abdullah reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) commanded a Muhrim (one who is in the state of pilgrimage) to kill the snake at Mina  — Muslim 5837
Abdullah reported:While we were with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) in the cave, the rest of the hadith is the same as the one narrated above  — Muslim 5838
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying (that he who kills a gecko) with the first stroke there are seventy rewards for him  — Muslim 5848
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn 'Umar also  — Muslim 5854
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5856
Abu Huraira reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5857
Hammam b. Manabbih reported this hadith on the authority of Abu Huraira  — Muslim 5858
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:None of you should say: Woe be upon the Time, for verily Allah is the Time  — Muslim 5865
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Do not curse Time, for it is Allah Who is Time  — Muslim 5866
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not use the word karm (for wine) for worthy of respect is the heart of a believer  — Muslim 5868
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Do not name grape as karm, for worthy of respect is a Muslim  — Muslim 5869
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abia Mu'iwiya with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5879
Ubada b. as-Samit reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The vision of a believer is the forty-sixth part of Prophecy  — Muslim 5909
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Verily the vision of a believer is one of the forty-sixth part of Prophecy  — Muslim 5911
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The vision of a pious man is the forty-sixth part of Prophecy  — Muslim 5913
Ibn `Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The pious dreams are the seventieth part of Prophecy  — Muslim 5916
This hadith has been reported on the authority of `Ubaidullah with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5917
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:He who saw me in a dream in fact saw me, for the satan does not appear in my form  — Muslim 5919
Abu Qatada reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:He who saw me in dream in fact saw the truth (what is true)  — Muslim 5921
The above two hadith have been narrated likewise through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5922
Jabir b. `Abdullah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:He who saw me in a dream in fact saw me, for the satan cannot assume my form  — Muslim 5924
The above hadith was likewise narrated with another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5956
Abu Sa'id reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The similitude of mine and that of the Apostles; the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 5962
This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 5964
Jundab reported:I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying: I shall be there at the Cistern before you  — Muslim 5966
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jundab through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5967
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id Khudri through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5970
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5980
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hudhaifa through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5981
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 5987
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:I will drive away from my Cistern people just as the stray camels are driven away  — Muslim 5993
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through another chain ot transmitters  — Muslim 5994
Anas b. Malik reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was the best amongst people in disposition and behaviour  — Muslim 6017
Jabir b. 'Abdullah reported:It never happened that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked for anything and he said: No  — Muslim 6018
The above hadith has likewise been narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6029
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jarir through other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6031
This hadith had been narrated on the authority of al-A'mash through another chain of transmitters also  — Muslim 6034
Anas reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters, but he made no mention of a camel-driver having a melodious voice  — Muslim 6038
The above hadith has been narrated through several other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6046
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shibab through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6047
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham through another chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6049
A'isha reported:When revelation descended upon Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) even during the cold days, his forehead perspired  — Muslim 6058
Anas reported that the hair of Allah's Messenger (may. peace be upon him) came upon his shoulders  — Muslim 6068
Anas reported that the hair of Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) reached half of the earlobe  — Muslim 6069
Muhammad b. Sirin reported:I asked Anas b. Malik whether Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) dyed his hair. He said: He had but little white hair  — Muslim 6075
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Muthanna through the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6078
Anas (b. Malik) was asked about the old age of Allah's Apostle (ﷺ). He said:Allah did not blemish him with white hair  — Muslim 6079
Abu Juhaifa reported:I saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) that he had white complexion and had some white hair, and Hasan b. 'Ali resembled him  — Muslim 6081
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Juhaifa with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6082
Jabir. Samura reported:I saw the seal on his back as if it were a pigeon's egg  — Muslim 6085
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Simak with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6086
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Khalid with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6103
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6110
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Qatada  — Muslim 6124
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The crying of the child (starts) when the satan begins to prick him  — Muslim 6136
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas through a different chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6139
Anas reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6140
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6142
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through another chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6143
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ma'mar  — Muslim 6150
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Salama with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6152
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Zakariyya (peace be upon him) was a carpenter  — Muslim 6162
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Ishaq  — Muslim 6166
Ibn 'Abbas has reported this hadith on the authority of Ubayy b. ka'b that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) used to recite this  — Muslim 6167
Abdullah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:If I were to choose from my Umma anyone as my bosom friend, I would have chosen Abu Bakr  — Muslim 6173
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira through another chain of transrritters  — Muslim 6186
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Umar b. Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6188
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:I saw Ibn Abu Quhafa drawing (water) ; the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 6194
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sa'd b. Ibrahim with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6205
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Uthman and A'isha with the same wording  — Muslim 6211
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Musa al-Ash'ari with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6213
Sa'd reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying to 'Ali:Aren't you satisfied with being unto me what Aaron was unto Moses?  — Muslim 6221
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zaid b. Arqam through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6226
Abdullah b. 'Amir b. Rabi reported A'isha as saying:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) went to bed one night; the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 6232
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Ali through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6234
Sa'd b Abi Waqqqs said:Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) gathered his parents for me on the Day of Uhud  — Muslim 6235
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yabyl b. Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6236
Abu 'Uthman reported on one of the days when Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was fighting and none remained with him save Talha and Sa'd  — Muslim 6242
Jabir reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6244
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Abu Ishaq with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6255
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying to Hasan:O Allah, behold, I love him. Thou too love him and love one who loves him  — Muslim 6256
This hadith has been narrated on the authority 'Abdullah through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6263
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Habib b. Ash-Shahid  — Muslim 6267
This hadith has been narrated on the a tliority of Ibn Abi Aufa through other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6275
A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) gave glad tidings to Khadija bint Khuwailid of a palace in Paradise  — Muslim 6276
A'isha reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) did not marry any other woman till her (Khadija's) death  — Muslim 6281
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6288
A'isha reported that people sent their gifts when it was the turn of 'A'isha seeking thereby the pleasure of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)  — Muslim 6289
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6291
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6294

Miswar b. Makhramah reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Fatima is a part of me. He in fact tortures me who tortures her  — Muslim 6308
The above hadith is narrated likewise through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6327
Abu Musa. reported:I came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and thought that 'Abdullah was amongst the members of the family, or like that  — Muslim 6328
Zaid b. Wahab reported:I was sitting along with Hudhaifa and Abu Musa, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 6331
This tradition has been transmitted on the authority of Shu'ba through A'mash, but there is a difference of order of the four  — Muslim 6337
Jabir reported that the Throne of the most Compassionate shook because of the death of Sa'd b. Mu'adh  — Muslim 6346
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Shu'ba combining the two chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6350
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Anas through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6374

This hadith has been reported on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6419
Abu Usaid Ansari has reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters. When the Prophet (ﷺ) was marching towards Badr in order  — Muslim 6422
Anas reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) but he has made no mention in the hadith of the words of Sa'd  — Muslim 6423
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn Umar but through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6437

The other hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6452

Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) established fraternity between Abu Ubaida b. Jarrah and Abu Talha  — Muslim 6462
Anas reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) established fraternity between the Quraish and the Ansar in his house at Medina  — Muslim 6464
This hadith has been transmitted by Mansur on the authority of Abu al-Ahwas and Jarir with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6471
This hadith has been transmitted by Zuhri on the authority of Ma'mar  — Muslim 6480
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Sulaiman Taimi through other chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6484
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Habib with the several chains of transmitters  — Muslim 6505
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Let him be humbled thrice, and the rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 6512
Anas b. Malik reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying like this. This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6530
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:There should be no estranged relations beyond three days  — Muslim 6535

The above hadith has been transmitted by Hisham with the same chain  — Muslim 6564
Ibu 'Umar reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:Oppression is the darkness on the Day of Resurrection  — Muslim 6577
Abu Musa reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:A believer is like a brick for another believer, the one supporting the other  — Muslim 6585
Nu'man b. Bashir reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ)  — Muslim 6587
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Nu'man b. Bashir through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6590
This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn Munkadir with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6597
Jarir reported from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ):He who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good  — Muslim 6598
Jarir reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:He who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good  — Muslim 6599
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:It does not seem proper for a Siddiq that he should be an invoker of curse  — Muslim 6608
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Kuraib with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6609
Jabir reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) a hadith like it but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6617
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Zinad with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 6620

A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Humaid with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6650
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:When any one of you fights with his brother he should avoid striking at the face  — Muslim 6651
This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Zinad and he said:" When one amongst you strikes (at the face)  — Muslim 6652
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:When any one of you fights with his brother, he should spare his face  — Muslim 6653
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:When any one of you fights with his brother, he should not slap at the face  — Muslim 6654
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:When any one of you fights with his brother, he should avoid the face  — Muslim 6656
Abu Huraira reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6667
The above hadith is narrated through another chain of transmitters with the same meaning  — Muslim 6676
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Suhail with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 6684

A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of 'Abdullah  — Muslim 6719

This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Mu'tamir, reported on the authority of his father  — Muslim 6976

Asma' reported that Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) said:There is none more self-respecting than Allah, the Exalted and Glorious  — Muslim 6998

Ibn 'Abbas reported that the moon was split up during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him)  — Muslim 7079

Anas b. Malik reported:The Paradise is surrounded by hardships and the Hell-Fire is surrounded by temptations  — Muslim 7130

A'isha reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:He who is examined thoroughly In reckoning is undone  — Muslim 7228

Jabir reported:I was in the company of Ibn Mas'ud that there blew a red storm. The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 7282
Jabir reported:I was in the house of 'Abdullah b. Mas'ud and the house was fully packed that a red storm blew in Kufah  — Muslim 7283
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Ja'far with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 7300
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The Ka'ba would be destroyed by an Abyssinian having two small shanks  — Muslim 7305

This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Umm Salama that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to 'Ammar:A group of rebels would kill you  — Muslim 7322

Ma'qil b. Yasar reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:Worshiping during the period of widespread turmoil is like emigration towards me  — Muslim 7400
The previous hadith is narrated through Abu Kamil from Hammad with the same chain  — Muslim 7401
Abdullah reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying:The Last Hour would affect (most terribly) the wicked persons  — Muslim 7402
Anas reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) as saying like this, but he narrated it through another chain of transmitters  — Muslim 7407
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:The world is a prison-house for a believer and Paradise for a non-believer  — Muslim 7417
Jabir reported Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) narrating a hadith like this with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 7419
Mutarrif reported on the authority of his father:I went to Allah's Apostle (ﷺ). The rest of the hadith is the same  — Muslim 7421
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of al-'Ala' b. 'Abd al-Rahman with the same chain of transmitters  — Muslim 7423
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri as reported by Yunus with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 7426
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying:O Allah, make for the family of Muhammad the provision which is a bare subsistence  — Muslim 7440
A'isha reported:Never could the family of Muhammad (ﷺ) (afford to eat to the fill) the bread of wheat beyond three days (successively)  — Muslim 7446
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording  — Muslim 7467
Salama b. Kuhail reported:I heard from Jundub, but I did not hear him say this: "I heard Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) saying this  — Muslim 7479
